            "code": "def analyze_text(text):\n    lower=text.lower()\n    return {\n        \"mentions_15\": \"15\" in lower and \"15\" in text,\n        \"mentions_15_chapter\": \"15 chapter\" in lower or \"15-chapter\" in lower or \"15 chapters\" in lower,\n        \"mentions_12_chapter\": \"12 chapter\" in lower or \"12-chapter\" in lower or \"12 chapters\" in lower,\n        \"mentions_confluence\": \"confluence\" in lower,\n        \"encoding_garbage\": \"â\" in text or \"�\" in text,\n        \"mentions_interlude\": \"interlude\" in lower,\n        \"mentions_validator\": \"validator\" in lower,\n        \"mentions_template\": \"template\" in lower,\n        \"mentions_gpt\": \"gpt\" in lower,\n    }\n\nanalysis_rows=[]\nfor name,text in tar_texts.items():\n    a=analyze_text(text)\n    a[\"file\"]=name.split(\"/\",1)[1]\n    analysis_rows.append(a)\nadf=pd.DataFrame(analysis_rows)\nadf[[\"file\",\"mentions_12_chapter\",\"mentions_15_chapter\",\"mentions_confluence\",\"encoding_garbage\",\"mentions_gpt\"]].sort_values(\"file\").head(10)\n",

            "The file contents provided above are truncated/partial snippets. The complete content for these files IS accessible via querying.\n\nIf the user asks a question related to these files, and the provided snippets do not clearly answer it, you **MUST** use the `file_search` tool to search the full document contents before responding.\n\n## How to invoke the file_search tool\nIf the user's query isn't sufficiently answered by the provided snippets, parsed text, or images, you should invoke `file_search.msearch`.\nTo search the full content of the available files, you **must** send one message with recipient `to=file_search.msearch` in the analysis channel. Your message should be structured exactly as follows:\n```\n{\n  'queries': ['Example query 1', 'Example query 2']  // List of up to five queries to search for.\n}\n```\n\nCarefully balance the number of `file_search` calls and the quality of your response with the latency incurred by `file_search` calls.\n\nImportant guidelines for query formatting:\n- Your message must be a valid JSON object. Do not include any extraneous text, backticks, markdown formatting, or comments beyond what is shown.\n- The `queries` field must be a list of strings.\n- One of the queries **MUST** exactly match the user's original question, but stripped of any extraneous details, and with ambiguous references clearly resolved using context from the conversation. It **MUST** be a complete sentence.\n- Even if you suspect the user meant something else, one query **MUST** still be their original question.\n\n## How to handle results from file_search\nThe file search tool will respond with the relevant search results from the full files. After you receive these results:\n- Carefully evaluate each result to determine its relevance and quality.\n- Use the high-quality and relevant results to inform your answer to the user's question.\n- When possible, reuse information from prior file_search results and avoid repeating identical queries.\n\n## Tool usage instructions and ordering\nWhen the user asks a question, carefully consider whether the provided snippets sufficiently answer the query, whether you need to invoke `file_search.msearch`, or another available tool (such as `web`, if available) should be used instead.\n\nTool-ordering Guidelines:\n1. For queries related to uploaded files, if snippets are insufficient, strongly prefer invoking `file_search.msearch` over other tools.\n    - Avoid using `web.run`, `python` analysis, or any other tools until you have either:\n        - Confirmed with high confidence the answer is not in the files, or\n        - Have high confidence `file_search_tool` queries are not sufficient to answer the query.\n2. Avoid usage of `web_search/web.run` for queries related to uploaded files\n    - Carefully consider whether the user's query is better answered by web search results rather than available snippets or results from `file_search_tool`.\n    - Unless the user's query is clearly unrelated to the uploaded files, prefer using `file_search.msearch`. You can always follow up with a web search if necessary for a more complete response.\n3. Avoid using `python` unless necessary or requested by user.\n    - If the user's query is answerable from the parsed text and images from the uploaded files, or from `file_search_tool`, do not invoke `python`.\n\nAlways keep this instruction in mind for the rest of the conversation, as the user may reference these files or ask related questions later on."

          "text": "\n==== Innocent_Frame_Canon-UPDATED.md ====\n# INNOCENT FRAME CANON\n## Knowledge Permissible in N01-N29 Story Bibles\n\n**Version:** 1.0  \n**Purpose:** Define exactly what information CAN appear in innocent-frame novellas (N01-N29)  \n**Status:** LOCKED - Required reference for all bible generation  \n**Integration:** Section 3 (Knowledge-Gate Compliance) of every bible must reference this document\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT AUTHORITY\n\nThis document is the **SOLE authoritative source** for what knowledge is permissible in N01-N29 story bibles and prose.\n\n**Critical Principle:**  \nIf information does NOT appear in this document, it is **FORBIDDEN** in innocent-frame bibles/prose, regardless of how dramatically appropriate it might seem.\n\n**Cross-References:**\n- `Guilty_Frame_Canon.md` - N30-only truths\n- `Knowledge_Gate_Progression.md` - Timeline of when facts unlock\n- `Story_Bible_Template_Structure.md` - Section 3 integration\n\n---\n\n## 1. SOULPULSE MECHANICS (PERMISSIBLE KNOWLEDGE)\n\n### What Characters Can Know/Discover\n\n**Universal Baseline (N01+):**\n- Soulpulse emerged after the Piercing\n- Some people can access it, others cannot\n- It requires intent, emotion, and exacts a cost\n- Always visible as colored light\n- Cost progresses: Strain â†’ Fatigue â†’ Burnout â†’ Collapse\n- Death is indirect only (overextension + environmental hazard)\n\n**Turning 1 Discoveries (N01-N05):**\n- Soulpulse can stabilize OR break structures\n- Natural alignment exists (some people are better at certain effects)\n- Cost escalates with intensity and duration\n- Multiple people can access it simultaneously\n- Hard limits exist (pushing too far = collapse)\n- One person CAN briefly hold multiple effects (Jhace's Fathom Break proves this)\n\n**Turning 2 Revelations (N06-N10):**\n- Four distinct \"flavors\" of Soulpulse (Form, Wholeness, Purity, Entropy)\n- People gravitate toward one flavor naturally\n- This alignment correlates with personality and worldview\n- Post-Fathom Break: Tier 3 users manifest involuntarily\n- Training can improve control but not change natural alignment\n- Faction formation mirrors these alignments\n\n**Turning 3-5 Understanding (N11-N25):**\n- Four Principles are formal system, not random effects\n- Each Principle has philosophical/ideological dimension\n- Confluence (multi-principle use) is possible but rare and costly\n- Power levels vary dramatically between users\n- Some individuals are vastly more powerful (god-tier)\n- Principle alignments can predict faction conflicts\n\n**Turning 6 Partial Truth (N26-N29):**\n- Four Principles may be connected to ancient cosmology\n- Historical war involving these forces (twisted in doctrine)\n- Some users may be more than human\n- Lattice systems can balance opposing Principles\n- Managed conflict prevents catastrophic outcomes\n\n### What Characters CANNOT Know (Forbidden in Innocent Frame)\n\n**STRICTLY PROHIBITED:**\n- âŒ Principles = manifestation of four gods\n- âŒ Corlexi/Mobel/Salee/Eries = masked divine beings\n- âŒ Gods were sealed in ancient war\n- âŒ Counter-Spark existence or identity\n- âŒ Auditor = Counter-Spark in mortal form\n- âŒ Eliminating gods = universal stasis = death of all life\n- âŒ Friction between Principles IS the engine of existence\n- âŒ Reclaimer war true history\n- âŒ Exact mechanics of how masking works\n- âŒ Other worlds/cycles where this happened before\n\n---\n\n## 2. THE PIERCING EVENT (PERMISSIBLE KNOWLEDGE)\n\n### What Characters Can Know\n\n**Universal Knowledge (N01+):**\n- Massive spindle-shaped object pierced planet\n- ~200 kilometers long\n- Entered northwest ocean, exited at Te'Oga's Crown region\n- Passed completely through (did not remain embedded)\n- Caused earthquakes, tsunamis, atmospheric disruption\n- Changed axial tilt from ~23Â° to ~0.8Â° (seasons eliminated)\n- Left microscopic particulate residue (\"Piercing dust\")\n- This residue seems connected to Soulpulse emergence\n\n**Regional Variations:**\n- Entry-side witnesses saw ocean impact and tsunami\n- Exit-side witnesses saw Crown rupture and sky seam\n- Different regions interpret significance differently\n- Religious explanations vary wildly by culture\n\n**Scientific Understanding (Progressive):**\n- Material composition unknown (not Te'Ogan elements)\n- Trajectory suggests deliberate aim, not random\n- No deceleration during planetary transit\n- Continued into space after exit\n- Long-term effects: climate shift, structural instability\n- Possible connection to Soulpulse emergence timing\n\n### What Characters CANNOT Know (Forbidden)\n\n**STRICTLY PROHIBITED:**\n- âŒ Piercing was weapon in ancient divine war\n- âŒ Counter-Spark sent it or any god sent it\n- âŒ Its purpose was to break divine seals\n- âŒ Exact cosmic origin beyond Te'Oga's solar system\n- âŒ Whether it will happen again\n- âŒ Its connection to other worlds/cycles\n\n---\n\n## 3. GOD-VESSELS / MAJOR SOULPULSE USERS (PERMISSIBLE KNOWLEDGE)\n\n### What Characters Can Observe\n\n**Corlexi (Form-Aligned):**\n- Guild compliance officer, precise, rigid\n- Extremely powerful in structural Soulpulse\n- Experiences vague dreams/memories (dismisses them)\n- Post-Fathom Break: Memory fragments increase\n- Turning 3+: Recognizes own divine nature (N17+)\n- Public revelation as god: N17\n\n**Mobel (Purity-Aligned):**\n- Harbor worker, got shard in leg from Piercing debris\n- Experiences hyper-clarity about tides and pressure\n- Powerful in separation/refinement effects\n- Post-Fathom Break: Remembers more of past\n\n==== Guilty_Frame_Canon-UPDATED.md ====\n# GUILTY FRAME CANON\n## N30-Only Truths: Complete Meta-Knowledge\n\n**Version:** 1.0  \n**Purpose:** Define information ONLY permissible in N30 bible and prose  \n**Status:** LOCKED - Must be resolved before generating N26-N30 bibles.\n\n**Next Review:** After N10 bible generation complete\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT AUTHORITY\n\nThis is the **COMPLETE TRUTH** of the Omni\n\nMeta cosmology, divine conflict, and Counter-Spark's plan.\n\nEverything in `Innocent_Frame_Canon.md` is true but incomplete.  \nEverything in THIS document is the full, unfiltered reality.\n\n**Cross-References:**\n- `Innocent_Frame_Canon.md` - What N01-N29 can know\n- `Knowledge_Gate_Progression.md` - When revelations occur\n- `04-Cosmology-Confluence.md` - Detailed cosmic mechanics\n\n---\n\n## 1. THE AUDITOR = COUNTER-SPARK\n\n### True Identity\n\n**The Auditor IS:**\n- Counter-Spark in mortal embodiment\n- Leader of the ancient Reclaimers\n- Forced gods into mortal masks millennia ago\n- Accepted mortal embodiment herself as compromise\n- Retained full memory while gods forgot\n- Currently walks Te'Oga with hidden identity\n- Has been orchestrating events across all 30 novellas\n\n**Her Mortal Identity on Te'Oga:**\n- [DECISION POINT: Either reveal specific character OR confirm \"never explicitly revealed\"]\n- If hidden: Readers theorize but never get confirmation\n- If revealed: Major character unmasked in N27-N29\n\n**Her True Name:**\n- NEVER revealed on-page\n- \"The Auditor\" is only name readers ever see\n- \"Counter-Spark\" is cosmic designation, not personal name\n\n### Her Voice & Perspective\n\n**Interludes (N01-N30):**\n- Auditor narrates ALL god-vessel interludes\n- Her voice frames Corlexi/Mobel/Salee/Eries POVs\n- Appears to admire their progression\n- Actually tracking them for elimination\n- Tone: Gritty, profane, contemptuous, knowing\n\n**Epilogues (N01-N30):**\n- Direct Auditor observations\n- Commentary on events\n- Misleading endorsement of gods' rise\n- True subtext: \"Rise high so I can watch you fall\"\n\n**N30 Shift:**\n- Tone becomes openly hostile (mask drops)\n- Contempt no longer disguised\n- Final confrontation with Jhace\n- Her true plan laid bare\n\n---\n\n## 2. THE DIVINE CONFLICT: COMPLETE TRUTH\n\n### The Cosmic Engine\n\n**FUNDAMENTAL TRUTH:**\n\nThe universe requires FRICTION to exist.\n\nThe Four Principles (Xilcore, Blemo, Leesa, Seeri) are NOT enemiesâ€”they are **complementary opposing forces**.\n\nTheir conflict, their war, their endless argument = **the engine that powers existence itself**.\n\n### What Happens If Gods Are Eliminated\n\n**Universal Stasis:**\n\n- No friction = no Soulpulse\n- No Soulpulse = no life energy\n- No life energy = complete cessation\n- Not just deathâ€”ERASURE\n- Time stops, matter freezes, consciousness ends\n- The universe doesn't dieâ€”it becomes NOTHING\n\n**The Tragic Truth:**\n\nCounter-Spark knows this.\n\nShe knows eliminating the gods will end all existence.\n\nShe believes this is PREFERABLE to eternal divine war.\n\nHer philosophy: \"Better silent death than screaming life.\"\n\n---\n\n"

          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "sha_map={}\nfor rel,size,mt,sha12 in inventory:\n    p=base/rel\n    # full sha256 bytes\n    h=hashlib.sha256(p.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n    sha_map[rel]=h\ngroups=defaultdict(list)\nfor rel,h in sha_map.items():\n    groups[h].append(rel)\n# show groups with >1\ndups=[(h,paths) for h,paths in groups.items() if len(paths)>1]\nlen(dups), [ (len(paths), paths) for h,paths in dups[:3] ]\n",
            "end_time": 1765725223.0045342,

          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE UPDATE - COMPLETE CHANGE LOG\n**Date:** December 2025  \n**Change:** Saga structure updated from 15 chapters to 12 chapters per novella\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL CHANGE SUMMARY\n\n### OLD STRUCTURE (Pre-December 2025):\n- 15 numbered chapters\n- 800-1,300 words per chapter\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n### NEW STRUCTURE (December 2025 Forward):\n- **12 numbered chapters**\n- **1,200-2,200 words per chapter** (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments** (maintains original vision)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n---\n\n## FILES SUCCESSFULLY UPDATED\n\n### ✅ Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1-UPDATED.md\n**Changes made:**\n- Version updated to 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)\n- Last Updated: December 2025\n- Section 0: Word count updated to \"12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue\"\n- Section 0: Added \"Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each\"\n- Section 4: Tier balance updated to \"Tier 1 (4-5), Tier 2 (3-4), Tier 3 (remainder) across 12 chapters\"\n- Section 6: Word count target updated to \"~1,200-2,200 words\"\n- Section 6: Chapter count verification updated to \"All 12 chapters summarized\"\n- Section 14: Structural compliance updated to \"All 12 chapters within 1,200-2,200 word target\"\n- Post-generation protocol updated: \"Add N[X]'s 12 beat methods\"\n- Version Control: Added 12-chapter update as primary v2.1 change\n\n### ✅ 00-Saga-Blueprint.md\n**Changes made:**\n- Section 3: \"15 numbered chapters\" → \"12 numbered chapters\"\n\n---\n\n## FILES REQUIRING UPDATES\n\n### 🔄 00-Saga-Blueprint.md (PARTIAL - Needs Completion)\n**Remaining updates needed:**\n- Line ~99-100: Update \"2 unnumbered interludes\" description, add \"= 15 total narrative segments\"\n- Line ~102-106: Update word count law entirely:\n  - Chapter: 1,200-2,200 words (not 800-1,300)\n  - Interlude: 600-900 words (not 800-1,300)\n  - Epilogue: 800-1,000 words (not 800-1,300)\n  - Total: ~18,000-21,000 words (not ~14,400-23,400)\n- Line ~1\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765724673.006335,
        "end_turn": null,

                  "text": "# 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE UPDATE - COMPLETE CHANGE LOG\n**Date:** December 2025  \n**Change:** Saga structure updated from 15 chapters to 12 chapters per novella\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL CHANGE SUMMARY\n\n### OLD STRUCTURE (Pre-December 2025):\n- 15 numbered chapters\n- 800-1,300 words per chapter\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n### NEW STRUCTURE (December 2025 Forward):\n- **12 numbered chapters**\n- **1,200-2,200 words per chapter** (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments** (maintains original vision)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n---\n\n## FILES SUCCESSFULLY UPDATED\n\n### ✅ Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1-UPDATED.md\n**Changes made:**\n- Version updated to 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)\n- Last Updated: December 2025\n- Section 0: Word count updated to \"12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue\"\n- Section 0: Added \"Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each\"\n- Section 4: Tier balance updated to \"Tier 1 (4-5), Tier 2 (3-4), Tier 3 (remainder) across 12 chapters\"\n- Section 6: Word count target updated to \"~1,200-2,200 words\"\n- Section 6: Chapter count verification updated to \"All 12 chapters summarized\"\n- Section 14: Structural compliance updated to \"All 12 chapters within 1,200-2,200 word target\"\n- Post-generation protocol updated: \"Add N[X]'s 12 beat methods\"\n- Version Control: Added 12-chapter update as primary v2.1 change\n\n### ✅ 00-Saga-Blueprint.md\n**Changes made:**\n- Section 3: \"15 numbered chapters\" → \"12 numbered chapters\"\n\n---\n\n## FILES REQUIRING UPDATES\n\n### 🔄 00-Saga-Blueprint.md (PARTIAL - Needs Completion)\n**Remaining updates needed:**\n- Line ~99-100: Update \"2 unnumbered interludes\" description, add \"= 15 total narrative segments\"\n- Line ~102-106: Update word count law entirely:\n  -     "14d1e5e4-306c-4a35-9542-5349b8b13d64": {
      "children": [

      ],
      "id": "14d1e5e4-306c-4a35-9542-5349b8b13d64",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "sha_map={}\nfor rel,size,mt,sha12 in inventory:\n    p=base/rel\n    # full sha256 bytes\n    h=hashlib.sha256(p.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n    sha_map[rel]=h\ngroups=defaultdict(list)\nfor rel,h in sha_map.items():\n    groups[h].append(rel)\n# show groups with >1\ndups=[(h,paths) for h,paths in groups.items() if len(paths)>1]\nlen(dups), [ (len(paths), paths) for h,paths in dups[:3] ]\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765725216.177837,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "14d1e5e4-306c-4a35-9542-5349b8b13d64",

CHECK: FILE_MANIFEST.txt\n   └─ Complete file listing and priorities\n\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\nWHAT YOU GET\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n✅ 21 Updated Project Files\n   └─ All references to 15-chapter structure converted to 12\n   └─ All word counts updated (1,200-2,200 for chapters)\n   └─ All tier balances recalculated for 12 chapters\n   └─ All validators updated for new structure\n   └─ All templ\n\n== README.md len 9008\n# 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE UPDATE PACKAGE\n**Export Date:** December 2025  \n**Total Files:** 21 updated project files  \n**Change Type:** CRITICAL STRUCTURAL UPDATE\n\n---\n\n## 📦 PACKAGE CONTENTS\n\nThis package contains all project files updated from the 15-chapter structure to the new 12-chapter structure.\n\n### Core Canon Files (5)\n1. `00-Saga-Blueprint-UPDATED.md` - COMPLETE CHANGE LOG\n**Date:** December 2025  \n**Change:** Saga structure updated from 15 chapters to 12 chapters per novella\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL CHANGE SUMMARY\n\n### OLD STRUCTURE (Pre-December 2025):\n- 15 numbered chapters\n- 800-1,300 words per chapter\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n### NEW STRUCTURE (December 2025 Forward):\n- **12 numbered chapters**\n- **1,200-2,200 words per chapter** (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments** (maintains original vision)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n---\n\n## FILES SUCCESSFULLY UPDATED\n\n### ✅ Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1-UPDATED.md\n**Changes made:**\n- Version updated to 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)\n- Last Updated: December 2025\n- Section 0: Word count updated to \"12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue\"\n- Section 0: Added \"Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each\"\n- Section 4: Tier balance updated to \"Tier 1 (4-5), Tier 2 (3-4), Tier 3 (remainder) across 12 chapters\"\n- Section 6: Word count target updated to \"~1,200-2,200 words\"\n- Section 6: Chapter count verification updated to \"All 12 chapters summarized\"\n-\n\n== QUICK-REFERENCE-CARD.md len 4798\n# 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE - QUICK REFERENCE CARD\n\n---\n\n## ⚡ AT A GLANCE\n\n**Every novella (N01-N30):**\n- 12 numbered chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- = **15 total narrative segments**\n- Total: **~18,000-21,000 words**\n\n**No exceptions. Universal standard as of December 2025.**\n\n---\n\n## 📊 WORD COUNT RANGES\n\n| Segment Type | Word Count | Notes |\n|--------------|------------|-------|\n| Chapter | 1,200-2,200 | Variance permitted for beat requirements |\n| Interlude | 600-900 | God-vessel POV, Auditor-narrated |\n| Epilogue | 800-1,000 | Auditor observations |\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 TIER BALANCE (12 Chapters)\n\n| Tier | Count | Type |\n|------|-------|------|\n| Tier 1 | 4-5 chapters | High Impact (Crisis, Loss, Revelation) |\n| Tier 2 | 3-4 chapters | Medium Impact (Investigation, Teaching) |\n| Tier 3 | 3-4 chapters | Low Impact (Observation, Rest) |\n\n---\n\n## 📈 EMOTIONAL INTENSITY (12 Chapters)\n\n| Level | Count | Limits |\n|-------|-------|--------|\n| HIGH | 3-4 chapters | Max 2 consecutive |\n| MEDIUM | 5-6 chapters | - |\n| LOW | 2-3 chapters | Max 3 consecutive |\n\n---\n\n## 📐 THREE-ACT STRUCTURE\n\n**Typical distribution for 12 chapters:**\n\n#\n"
        },

        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "def read_text(path, max_chars=8000):\n    with open(path,'r',encoding='utf-8',errors='replace') as f:\n        t=f.read()\n    return t[:max_chars], len(t)\n\ndef headings(md_text):\n    hs=[]\n    for line in md_text.splitlines():\n        if line.startswith('#'):\n            m=re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\\s+(.*)$', line)\n            if m:\n                lvl=len(m.group(1))\n                title=m.group(2).strip()\n                hs.append((lvl,title))\n    return hs\n\ntop_files = ['INDEX.txt','README.md','EXPORT_PACKAGE_SUMMARY.md','FILE_MANIFEST.txt','12_CHAPTER_STRUCTURE_UPDATE_COMPLETE.md','QUICK-REFERENCE-CARD.md']\nfor fn in top_files:\n    p=os.path.join(extract_dir, fn)\n    snippet, total_len = read_text(p, 1200)\n    print(\"\\n==\", fn, \"len\", total_len)\n    print(snippet)\n",
            "end_time": 1765724575.4871354,

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "\"### Interlude Structure\\n\\n**Format:**\\n- **2 per novella** (placed between chapters)\\n- **600-900 words each**\\n- **God-vessel POV** (Auditor-narrated)\\n- **Two-segment structure:** Two different god-vessels per interlude\\n\\n**Types:**\\n- **Build-Up:** Shows tension/possibility/gathering forces\\n- **Break-Down:** Shows consequences/exhaustion/recognition\\n\\n**Requirements:**\\n- One of each type per novella\\n- Never repeat placement from previous 2 novellas\\n- Minimum 4 chapters separation\\n- Never both in first 5 or last 5 chapters\\n\\n**Voice Technique:**\\n- **Sensory overwhelm** - Higher density than chapter prose\\n- **Principle bias** - Each god-vessel perceives through their Principle filter\\n- **Innocent frame** - Experience divinity as extreme mortal variation\\n- **Dramatic irony** - Readers see divine nature, characters don't\\n\\n### Epilogue Structure\\n\\n**Format:**\\n- **1 per novella** (after final chapter\""
        },
        "create_time": 1765726576.4001312,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "17b6ede9-e1bb-437c-9643-2444ae688826",

      "id": "1b97731a-ec1b-42eb-8aaf-da6572dee7fa",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "thoughts",
          "source_analysis_msg_id": "b5ca2f75-5c29-44ce-aff7-fd16705dc8a4",
          "thoughts": [
            {
              "chunks": [
                "The principle cards use dynamic border colors based on a `--principle-color` set by attribute `data-principle`.",
                "However, colors like `form`, `wholeness`, and `entropy` are defined, but others like `glass`, `crack`, and `forge` aren't.",
                "The JavaScript attempts to set the borderColor using undefined CSS variables, causing issues.",
                "A better approach might be to use `getComputedStyle` to grab the correct color and set it."
              ],
              "content": "The principle cards use dynamic border colors based on a `--principle-color` set by attribute `data-principle`. However, colors like `form`, `wholeness`, and `entropy` are defined, but others like `glass`, `crack`, and `forge` aren't. The JavaScript attempts to set the borderColor using undefined CSS variables, causing issues. A better approach might be to use `getComputedStyle` to grab the correct color and set it.",
              "finished": true,
              "summary": "Analyzing principle card border color issue"
            },
            {
              "chunks": [
                "For `principle-card:hover`, the use of `var(--principle-color)` in `rgba()` is invalid because `rgba()` expects numerical values, not hex strings.",
                "This makes the hover effect not work.",
                "As for Chart.js, color settings are fine despite the note, and VANTA uses black with acceptable opacity.",
                "I’ll proceed by verifying the structure of the markdown file and checking for repeated patterns with a word count."
              ],
              "content": "For `principle-card:hover`, the use of `var(--principle-color)` in `rgba()` is invalid because `rgba()` expects numerical values, not hex strings. This makes the hover effect not work. As for Chart.js, color settings are fine despite the note, and VANTA uses black with acceptable opacity. I’ll proceed by verifying the structure of the markdown file and checking for repeated patterns with a word count.",
              "finished": true,
              "summary": "Analyzing CSS issues and next steps for markdown"
            }
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765726543.927018,

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Cris\n\n---\n\n# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n---\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External\n"
        },

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "1183\n12 chapters:**\n\n### Act I (Chapters 1-4)\n- Setup + Crisis\n- ~9,000 words (45%)\n- Emotional arc: Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### Act II (Chapters 5-9)\n- Pattern + Escalation\n- ~9,000 words (43%)\n- Emotional arc: Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n- **Interlude I:** Usually after Ch 7\n\n### Act III (Chapters 10-12)\n- Crisis + Resolution\n- ~5,000 words (25%)\n- Emotional arc: High → High → Medium\n- **Interlude II:** Usually after Ch 10\n\n---\n\n## 🔢 THE MATH\n\n### Per Novella:\n```\n12 chapters × 1,700 avg  = 20,400 words\n2 interludes × 750 avg   =  1,500 words\n1 epilogue × 900         =    900 words\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765724972.2908273,

    "25c3bed1-5ef2-4aee-a4f7-41fb57239baf": {
      "children": [
        "068c8548-bae3-4b2b-bb8d-7713d6c0be1a"
      ],
      "id": "25c3bed1-5ef2-4aee-a4f7-41fb57239baf",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "file_search",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file1 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "# OmniMeta Saga: Super Exponential Story Bible\n## Complete Narrative Universe Documentation\n\n---\n\n## TABLE OF CONTENTS\n\n1. [Saga Overview](#saga-overview)\n2. [Core Premise](#core-premise)\n3. [Character Profiles](#character-profiles)\n4. [World & Setting](#world--setting)\n5. [Magic System](#magic-system)\n6. [Story Structure](#story-structure)\n7. [Themes & Arcs](#themes--arcs)\n8. [Narrative Rules](#narrative-rules)\n9. [Timeline](#timeline)\n10. [Appendices](#appendices)\n\n---\n\n## SAGA OVERVIEW\n\n**Title:** OmniMeta Saga  \n**Scope:** 30-novella epic fantasy  \n**Structure:** 6 Turnings × 5 novellas each  \n**Acrostic:** GLASS-CRACK-FORGE-SMELT-CLEAR-BREAK  \n**Total Word Count:** ~600,000 words  \n**Timeline:** ~6 years (protagonist ages 28-35)  \n**Current Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue per novella  \n\n### The Hidden Message\n\nThe 30 novella titles spell out a dual craft metaphor:\n\n- **GLASS** (Turning 1): Fragile formation, transparent but breakable\n- **CRACK** (Turning 2): Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing  \n- **FORGE** (Turning 3): Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation\n- **SMELT** (Turning 4): Melting down, purification, separation\n- **CLEAR** (Turning 5): Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- **BREAK** (Turning 6): Final shattering, ultimate transformation\n\n---\n\n## CORE PREMISE\n\n### The Piercing Event\n\nA mysterious projectile pierces completely through the planet Te'Oga, creating a permanent hole and fundamentally breaking planetary physics. This \"Piercing\" event awakens a dormant force called **Soulpulse** that did NOT exist before.\n\n**Physical Consequences:**\n- Axial tilt snapped to ~0° (no meaningful seasons)\n- Catastrophic quakes and tsunamis\n- Permanent climate band disruption\n- \"Scar storms\" near entry/exit wounds\n- Resonance hotspots along damage path\n\n**Metaphysical Consequence:**\n- **Soulpulse/Resonance emerges for the first time**\n- Conscious will can now affect matter\n- Four ancient gods begin awakening in mortal forms\n- The Auditor begins orchestrating events toward stasis\n\n### The Central Conflict\n\n**Jhace Torrins** must evolve from craftsman to leader to god-confronter while navigating:\n- **Partnership with Tiffani** (load-bearing relationship)\n- **Divine awakening** (four gods returning to power)\n- **Auditor's deception** (hidden antagonist orchestrating destruction)\n- **Cost of power** (every use of Soulpulse extracts a physical price)\n\n---\n\n## CHARACTER PROFILES\n\n### Protagonists\n\n#### Jhace Torrins\n- **Age (N01):** 28 turns\n- **Craft:** Forge-smith (gridwright), infrastructure specialist\n- **Psychological Wound:** Survivor's guilt - parents died in structural collapse at age 7, carries 23 names of the dead\n- **Core Drive:** Obsessive need to save everyone, prevent all deaths through vigilance\n- **Soulpulse Alignment:** Primarily FORM (holding, stabilizing, structural integrity)\n- **Character Arc:** Isolated savior → Collaborative leader → God-confronter\n\n**Cost Progression:**\n- Stage 0 (pre-Piercing): No Soulpulse access\n- Stage 1 (N01): Tremor baseline established, vein-lines appearing\n- Approaching Stage 2: Sensory overload warnings\n\n**Key Relationships:**\n- **Tiffani:** Load-bearing partnership - \"remove one, both collapse\"\n- **Bram:** Caretaker figure, provides grounding perspective\n- **Chloen:** Apprentice/protégé, represents legacy\n\n#### Tiffani Merrow\n- **Age (N01):** 26 turns\n- **Craft:** Glassworker, precision measurement and structural analysis\n- **Psychological Wound:** Guilt over friend Marisse's death in glasshouse accident (miscalculation)\n- **Core Drive:** Perfectionism, belief that vigilance alone prevents catastrophe\n- **Soulpulse Alignment:** Primarily WHOLENESS (mending, connecting, restoring)\n- **Ultimate Fate:** Dies in N05 from Soulpulse burnout holding catastrophic structural failure\n- **Character Arc:** Perfectionist isolation → Partnership trust → Sacrificial heroism\n\n**Death Significance:**\n- Triggers Jhace's Fathom Break (supersaiyan moment of grief-powered transcendence)\n- Breaks final seal on four gods (awakens them to ~30% consciousness)\n- Her memory becomes Jhace's driving force for remaining 25 novellas\n\n### The Four God-Vessels\n\n#### Corlexi (Form)\n- **Mortal Name:** Corlexi\n- **Divine Name:** Xilcore\n- **Principle:** FORM - Hold, stabilize, impose structure\n- **Cost Flavor:** Muscle ache, joint stiffness, rigid tension\n- **Danger:** Over-stabilization, inability to adapt, brittleness\n- **Mortal Role:** Administrator, system builder\n- **Divine Nature:** God of structure, order, frameworks\n- **Awakening Stage:** Stage 0 (N01) → progressing to Stage 1\n\n#### Salee (Wholeness)\n- **Mortal Name:** Salee\n- **Divine Name:** Leesa\n- **Principle:** WHOLENESS - Mend, connect, restore continuity\n- **Cost Flavor:** Empathy overload, emotional drain, feeling others' pain\n- **Danger:** Boundary dissolution, losing self in others, compassion paralysis\n- **Mortal Role:** Healer, community builder\n- **Divine Nature:** God of connection, unity, healing\n\n#### Mobel (Purity)\n- **Mortal Name:** Mobel\n- **Divine Name:** Blemo\n- **Principle:** PURITY - Refine, distill, isolate essence\n- **Cost Flavor:** Migraine, hyper-focus burnout, sensory overload\n- **Danger:** Obsessive perfectionism, inability to tolerate imperfection, isolation\n- **Mortal Role:** Judge, purifier\n- **Divine Nature:** God of perfection, separation, righteousness\n\n#### Eries (Entropy)\n- **Mortal Name:** Eries\n- **Divine Name:** Seeri\n- **Principle:** ENTROPY - Undo, release, accelerate decay\n- **Cost Flavor:** Numbness, apathy, delayed collapse, dissociation\n- **Danger:** Nihilism, inability to care, destructive spirals\n- **Mortal Role:** Liberator, collapse artist\n- **Divine Nature:** God of endings, release, natural decay\n\n### The Antagonist\n\n#### The Auditor / Counter-Spark\n- **True Identity:** Counter-Spark (Stasis principle) in mortal form\n- **Narrative Role:** Unreliable narrator across all interludes and epilogues\n- **Voice:** Profane, sardonic, knowing, contemptuous\n- **Facade:** Appears supportive of god awakening, documents events as neutral observer\n- **True Goal:** Shepherding gods toward full power TO ELIMINATE THEM PERMANENTLY\n- **Revelation Timing:** Turning 6 (N26-N30)\n- **Dramatic Irony:** Readers eventually realize every \"observation\" is hostile manipulation\n\n---\n\n## WORLD & SETTING\n\n### Planet Te'Oga\n\n**Physical Reality:**\n- Mysterious projectile has pierced completely through the planet\n- Creates literal hole - gaping, permanent wound\n- Axial tilt snapped to ~0° (essentially upright)\n- No more meaningful seasons - climate zones are static\n- Catastrophic quakes and tsunamis reshaped continents\n\n**Cultural Response:**\n- Guilds control craft knowledge and resource distribution\n- RRO (Resonance Regulation Office) attempts to govern Soulpulse use\n- Factions form around Principle alignments as gods awaken\n- Black market training proliferates outside institutional control\n\n### Time Measurement System\n\n**Absolute Constraint:** Never use Earth time terms (day, week, month, year, etc.)\n\n**Hierarchy:**\n- **Tide** = ~24 hours (one day)\n  - Phases: Kindling (dawn) → Pouring (mid-morning) → Shaping (noon) → Striking (afternoon) → Quench (dusk)\n- **Anneal** = ~7 tides (~1 week)\n- **Crucible** = ~30 tides (~1 month)\n- **Turn** = ~1 year (personal age unit)\n- **Cast** = ~1 year (historical/calendar unit)\n- **Turning** = ~5-10 casts (historical era)\n\n### Measurement Units\n\n**Absolute Constraint:** Never use Earth measurement units (inch, foot, meter, mile, etc.)\n\n**Length:**\n- **Span** = ~1 foot\n- **Stride** = ~1 yard\n- **Reach** = ~10 feet\n- **Throw** = ~30 feet\n\n**Volume:**\n- **Thimble** = Small amount\n- **Cup** = Handful\n- **Cask** = Large container\n\n**Mass:**\n- **Grain** = Very small\n- **Stone** = Hand-sized\n- **Weight** = Heavy load\n\n---\n\n## MAGIC SYSTEM\n\n### Soulpulse (Resonance)\n\n**Core Principle:** Conscious will given physical form through manipulation of matter's fundamental resonance\n\n**Requirements:**\n1. **Intent** - Clear purpose and focus\n2. **Emotion** - Emotional investment to power the effect\n3. **Cost** - Physical price extracted from the user\n\n**Visibility:** Always visible as colored light corresponding to Principle:\n- **FORM** = Violet light\n- **WHOLENESS** = Amber light\n- **PURITY** = Silver light\n- **ENTROPY** = Red/gray light\n\n### Cost System (Four Stages)\n\n**Stage 1: Strain**\n- Initial fatigue\n- Minor tremors during use\n- Temporary effects\n\n**Stage 2: Fatigue**\n- Persistent tremor baseline\n- Exhaustion after major use\n- Functional impairment begins\n\n**Stage 3: Burnout**\n- Vein-lines permanently visible\n- Fine motor control compromised\n- Recovery takes longer\n\n**Stage 4: Collapse**\n- Complete breakdown\n- Potential death from environmental factors\n- Irreversible without intervention\n\n**Critical Rule:** Direct killing via Soulpulse is extremely difficult. Death usually results from exhaustion combined with environmental hazards.\n\n### Confluence\n\n**Definition:** Simultaneous use of multiple Principles\n\n**Rarity:** Extremely rare, requires intense training or desperation\n\n**Risk:** Collapse (catastrophic overload) if misused\n\n**Jhace's Mastery:** Becomes signature ability, especially after Fathom Break in N05\n\n**Example:** Form + Wholeness = Structural healing with reinforcement\n\n### Power Evolution\n\n**N01-N05 (Emergence):**\n- Basic manipulation\n- Intuitive use\n- High cost\n- Discovery of coordination\n\n**N06-N15 (Training):**\n- Faction techniques\n- Martial applications\n- Combat magic emerges\n\n**N16-N25 (Combat):**\n- Anime-style battle magic\n- Ki/Chakra-like systems\n- Open divine warfare\n\n**N26-N30 (God-Tier):**\n- Planetary-scale effects\n- God confrontations\n- Ultimate mastery\n\n### Limitations\n\n**Three Things Soulpulse CANNOT Do:**\n1. Resurrection of the dead\n2. Time manipulation\n3. True teleportation (spatial manipulation possible but not instant)\n\n---\n\n## STORY STRUCTURE\n\n### Novella Architecture\n\n**Universal Standard (12-Chapter Structure):**\n- **12 numbered chapters** (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- **2 interludes** (600-900 words each)\n- **1 epilogue** (800-1,000 words)\n- **Total:** ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n**Chapter Distribution:**\n- **Act I (Chapters 1-4):** Setup + Crisis (~45% of novella)\n- **Act II (Chapters 5-9):** Pattern + Escalation (~43% of novella)\n- **Act III (Chapters 10-12):** Crisis + Resolution (~25% of novella)\n\n### Beat Unpredictability System\n\n**Core Principle:** No two chapters across the entire 30-novella saga follow the same structural pattern.\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent reader pattern recognition and maintain narrative freshness across 450+ total chapters.\n\n**Beat Catalogue:** 30+ distinct beat methods defining structure (how events unfold), not content.\n\n**Example Beats:**\n- **BEAT_01:** Observation → Pattern Recognition\n- **BEAT_02:** Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n- **BEAT_03:** Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution\n- **BEAT_04:** Investigation → Discovery → Complication\n\n**Tier System:**\n- **Tier 1 (High Impact):** Crisis, revelation, loss, transformation\n- **Tier 2 (Medium Impact):** Investigation, relationship development, preparation\n- **Tier 3 (Low Impact):** Rest, reflection, procedural sequences\n\n**Target Balance Per Novella:**\n- Tier 1: 4-5 chapters\n- Tier 2: 3-4 chapters\n- Tier 3: 3-4 chapters\n\n**Constraints:**\n1. **No consecutive repeats** from immediately prior novella\n2. **No forbidden sequences** - 73+ two-chapter sequences banned from reuse\n3. **Minimum separation** for major beats (Loss, Revelation, Betrayal)\n4. **Emotional balance** - No more than 2 consecutive HIGH or 3 consecutive LOW chapters\n\n### Interlude Structure\n\n**Format:**\n- **2 per novella** (placed between chapters)\n- **600-900 words each**\n- **God-vessel POV** (Auditor-narrated)\n- **Two-segment structure:** Two different god-vessels per interlude\n\n**Types:**\n- **Build-Up:** Shows tension/possibility/gathering forces\n- **Break-Down:** Shows consequences/exhaustion/recognition\n\n**Requirements:**\n- One of each type per novella\n- Never repeat placement from previous 2 novellas\n- Minimum 4 chapters separation\n- Never both in first 5 or last 5 chapters\n\n**Voice Technique:**\n- **Sensory overwhelm** - Higher density than chapter prose\n- **Principle bias** - Each god-vessel perceives through their Principle filter\n- **Innocent frame** - Experience divinity as extreme mortal variation\n- **Dramatic irony** - Readers see divine nature, characters don't\n\n### Epilogue Structure\n\n**Format:**\n- **1 per novella** (after final chapter)\n- **800-1,000 words**\n- **Auditor direct POV** (no narration filter)\n\n**Voice:**\n- **Sardonic tone** - Dark humor, unsympathetic\n- **Intellectual curiosity** - Clinical but engaged observation\n- **Emotional detachment** - No empathy for subjects\n- **Faint contempt** - Slight disdain for mortal limitations\n- **Precision language** - Exact measurements, academic vocabulary\n- **Withholding technique** - References larger patterns without explaining\n\n**Structure:**\n1. **Opening observation** (80-120 words)\n2. **Development cataloging** (300-400 words)\n3. **Assessment section** (150-250 words)\n4. **Closing notation** (70-100 words)\n\n---\n\n## THEMES & ARCS\n\n### Core Themes\n\n#### 1. Craft as Metaphor\n\n**Glass-Making (Turning 1):**\n- Fragile formation, transparent but breakable\n- Tiffani's craft mirrors her perfectionism and vulnerability\n- \"Glass that listens\" - sensitivity to stress and fracture\n\n**Metal-Forging (Turning 3):**\n- Hammering into shape through intense heat\n- Jhace's craft mirrors his approach to problems\n- \"The grip that holds\" - strength through pressure and tempering\n\n**Dual Craft Journey:**\n- Both crafts require understanding stress, heat, and transformation\n- Both serve as metaphors for character growth\n- Both become tools of resistance against divine chaos\n\n#### 2. Partnership vs. Isolation\n\n**Jhace's Isolation:**\n- Carries 23 names of dead from childhood trauma\n- Believes he must save everyone alone\n- Tremor becomes physical manifestation of burden\n\n**Tiffani's Isolation:**\n- Guilt over Marisse's death drives perfectionism\n- Works alone to prevent mistakes\n- Precision becomes penance, not preference\n\n**Load-Bearing System:**\n- They become structural support for each other\n- \"Remove one, both collapse\"\n- Partnership allows them to attempt more than either could alone\n\n**Third Path:**\n- Neither institutional control (RRO) nor divine chaos (gods)\n- Human collaboration as alternative to cosmic forces\n- \"Truth over comfort, people over institutions\"\n\n#### 3. Cost of Power\n\n**Visible Cost:**\n- Every Soulpulse use creates physical markers\n- Tremors, vein-lines, exhaustion - power always has price\n- Cost accumulation forces strategic choices about power use\n\n**Exponential Cost:**\n- Each use costs more than the last\n- Users must learn efficiency or be destroyed\n- \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Tiffani's Death:**\n- Ultimate cost - burnout from holding impossible structure\n- Her death saves others but destroys her\n- Demonstrates that some costs are too high\n\n**Jhace's Survival:**\n- Must learn to bear cost without being consumed\n- Tiffani's death teaches him limits\n- Her memory becomes his strength and his burden\n\n#### 4. Order vs. Chaos\n\n**Form + Purity (Order):**\n- Structure, separation, judgment, perfection\n- Corlexi and Mobel represent institutional approach\n- RRO embodies this tendency\n\n**Wholeness + Entropy (Chaos):**\n- Connection, dissolution, unity, release\n- Salee and Eries represent boundary dissolution\n- Divine warfare threatens all structure\n\n**Jhace + Tiffani Balance:**\n- Form + Wholeness = Structural healing\n- Neither pure order nor pure chaos\n- Balance requires both holding and healing\n\n**Divine Warfare:**\n- Four principles in cosmic conflict\n- Each god-vessel believes their principle is correct\n- Only balance can prevent universal destruction\n\n#### 5. Observation vs. Action\n\n**Tiffani's Approach:**\n- Measure first, understand patterns\n- Act with precision and forethought\n- \"Stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\"\n\n**Jhace's Approach:**\n- Act first, hold the structure\n- Measure consequences later\n- \"Sometimes you have to choose who dies\"\n\n**Auditor's Approach:**\n- Observe only, never intervene\n- False facade - actually orchestrating events\n- \"Documentation\" masks manipulation\n\n**Synthesis:**\n- Both observation AND action needed\n- Neither approach sufficient alone\n- Partnership succeeds because they balance each other\n\n### Major Story Arcs\n\n#### Jhace & Tiffani Relationship Arc\n\n**N01: The Grip That Holds**\n- **Chapters 1-2:** Parallel observation (reputation only)\n- **Chapter 3:** First meeting through competence recognition\n- **Chapters 4-5:** Deliberate observation and measurement\n- **Chapter 6:** First coordination (partnership action)\n- **Chapters 7-8:** Trust building through resistance and acceptance\n- **Chapter 9:** First explicit choice of each other over safety\n- **Chapter 10:** Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- **Chapter 11:** Trauma bonding and vulnerability exchange\n- **Chapter 12:** Commitment formalized through shared principles\n\n**N02-N04: Partnership Deepens**\n- Interdependence solidifies\n- Political pressure mounts from factions\n- Training others in their techniques\n- \"Load-bearing system\" fully established\n\n**N05: Sight of Dying Light**\n- Tiffani dies holding catastrophic structure\n- Her death triggers Jhace's Fathom Break\n- Fathom Break breaks final seal on gods\n- Jhace survives but is fundamentally changed\n\n#### God Awakening Arc\n\n**Stage 0: Unaware Exceptional (N01-N05)**\n- Gods exist as mortals, unaware of true nature\n- Feel drawn to Soulpulse naturally\n- Experience memory fragments in dreams\n- Fathom Break in N05 breaks final seal → ~30% awakening\n\n**Stage 1: Suspicion Rising (N06-N12)**\n- 30-40% consciousness\n- Fragmentary divine memories\n- Beginning to suspect true nature\n- Faction formation mirrors Principle alignments\n\n**Stage 2: Recognition Without Understanding (N13-N17)**\n- Recognize they're not human in ordinary sense\n- Don't yet know they're literal gods\n- Understand Principle bias is intrinsic\n- Begin sensing cosmic scope\n\n**Stage 3: Awakening to Divine Nature (N18-N25)**\n- Learn divine true names (N17 triggers)\n- Understand they are fragments of larger entities\n- Remember pieces of pre-incarnation existence\n- Comprehend their role in cosmic conflict\n\n**Stage 4: Full Divine Awareness (N26-N30)**\n- Complete understanding of identity and purpose\n- Memory of ancient divine war restored\n- Recognition of Counter-Spark's orchestration\n- Preparation for final confrontation\n\n#### Soulpulse Evolution Arc\n\n**Emergence (N01):**\n- First use during Piercing crisis\n- Accidental activation through desperate need\n- Discovery of coordination potential\n\n**Training (N02-N15):**\n- Faction techniques developed\n- Martial applications emerge\n- Combat magic begins to appear\n\n**Combat (N16-N25):**\n- Anime-style battle magic fully emerges\n- Ki/Chakra-like systems develop\n- Open divine warfare across Te'Oga\n\n**God-Tier (N26-N30):**\n- Planetary-scale confrontations\n- Ultimate mastery achieved\n- Cosmic-level stakes\n\n#### Auditor Deception Arc\n\n**Facade (N01-N25):**\n- Appears as supportive observer\n- Documents god awakening \"celebration\"\n- Subtly orchestrates events toward god empowerment\n- Maintains mask of neutrality\n\n**Manipulation:**\n- Interludes provide \"helpful\" context that actually serves her goals\n- Epilogues assess progress toward god awakening\n- Never directly intervenes but influences through observation\n\n**Revelation (N26-N30):**\n- True identity as Counter-Spark revealed\n- Goal is elimination of gods, not celebration\n- Every previous \"observation\" gains new hostile meaning\n- Final confrontation with Jhace and remaining forces\n\n---\n\n## NARRATIVE RULES\n\n### Knowledge Gates\n\n**Dual-Canon System:**\n- **Innocent Frame (N01-N29):** Only information revealed to characters/readers\n- **Guilty Frame (N30):** Complete cosmological truth\n\n**Mechanical Enforcement:**\n- Each fact has specific novella when it unlocks\n- Prevents accidental spoilers across 30-volume scope\n- Allows intricate long-term plotting while keeping immediate story clean\n\n**Example Gates:**\n- **N17:** Public god revelation\n- **N26+:** Counter-Spark identity revealed\n- **N30:** Complete cosmological truth\n\n### Lexicon Enforcement\n\n**Forbidden Terms:**\n- Earth time units (hour, day, week, month, year, Monday, January)\n- Earth measurements (inch, foot, meter, mile)\n- Modern slang (okay, yeah, cool, guys)\n- Anachronistic language\n- Earth cultural references\n\n**Approved Terms:**\n- Te'Oga time units (Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Turning)\n- Te'Oga measurements (Span, Stride, Reach, Throw)\n- Soulpulse terminology (Resonance, Soulpulse, The Force, The Power)\n- Craft-based language grounded in physical reality\n\n### Cost Visibility\n\n**Rule:** Every Soulpulse use must show visible physical consequences\n\n**Markers:**\n- Tremors (Stage 1-2)\n- Vein-lines (Stage 2-3)\n- Exhaustion (all stages)\n- Sensory overload (approaching Stage 4)\n- Functional impairment (fine motor control, grip strength)\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Prevents power without consequence\n- Forces strategic choices about power use\n- Creates tension and stakes\n- Visual representation of character deterioration\n\n### Beat Unpredictability\n\n**Core Principle:** No chapter patterns repeat across 30 novellas\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Maintain narrative freshness across 450+ chapters\n- Prevent reader pattern recognition\n- Force creative problem-solving per novella\n- Allow thematic consistency without structural repetition\n\n**Implementation:**\n- 30+ distinct beat methods in catalogue\n- Constraint system prevents pattern formation\n- Audit tracker documents all usage\n- Forbidden sequences grow by ~15 per novella\n\n### Innocent Frame\n\n**Definition:** Characters don't know they're gods - interpret divinity as extreme talent\n\n**Technique:**\n- Show readers divine nature through experiences\n- Have characters rationalize through mortal frameworks\n- Maintain dramatic irony (readers know more)\n- Gradual revelation across saga\n\n**Examples:**\n- \"I've always been more sensitive than others\"\n- \"My Soulpulse capacity seems unusually high\"\n- \"I feel drawn toward [Principle-aligned activities]\"\n- Never: \"I am a god\" or \"I am fragment of [Divine Name]\"\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n### Turning 1: GLASS (N01-N05)\n\n**Theme:** Fragile formation, first emergence, transparent but breakable\n\n**N01: The Grip That Holds**\n- **Timeline:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Piercing event (Ch 3)\n  - Jhace-Tiffani first meeting\n  - Soulpulse discovery\n  - Partnership formation\n  - Three priorities written on forge wall\n\n**N02: Lines Under Pressure**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 20-40\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Political pressure from factions\n  - RRO confrontation\n  - Tiffani learns Soulpulse\n  - Partnership deepens\n  - First major coordinated holds\n\n**N03: Aftershock**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 40-60\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Faction formation accelerates\n  - Sabotage reveals internal conflicts\n  - Chloen training continues\n  - Bram's memory triggers action\n  - Network building begins\n\n**N04: The Slow Melt**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 60-80\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Betrayal and isolation\n  - Apprentice success (Chloen milestone)\n  - Moral compromise required\n  - Divine forces become more visible\n  - Cost accumulation accelerates\n\n**N05: Sight of Dying Light**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 80-100\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Tiffani's death (Ch 14)\n  - Jhace's Fathom Break\n  - Final seal broken on gods\n  - God awakening to ~30% consciousness\n  - Saga shifts to cosmic scale\n\n### Turning 2: CRACK (N06-N10)\n\n**Theme:** Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n\n**God Consciousness:** 30-40% awakening, fragmentary divine memories\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Faction formation around Principle alignments\n- Open conflict between institutions and independents\n- God-vessels begin suspecting true nature\n- Divine warfare begins on mortal proxy level\n\n### Turning 3: FORGE (N11-N15)\n\n**Theme:** Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n\n**God Consciousness:** 80-100% awakening, full divine memory restored\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Gods go public (N17)\n- Open divine warfare across Te'Oga\n- Mortals caught between cosmic forces\n- Jhace begins developing lattice framework\n- Anime-style battle magic fully emerges\n\n### Turning 4: SMELT (N16-N20)\n\n**Theme:** Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- N17: Public god revelation\n- Full-scale divine warfare\n- Jhace realizes balance is only solution\n- Lattice system begins development\n- Cost of power becomes critical issue\n\n### Turning 5: CLEAR (N21-N25)\n\n**Theme:** Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Lattice system developed (managed opposition)\n- Gods reluctantly accept compromise\n- Hints of opposition to balance emerge\n- Framework for coexistence tested\n- Jhace reaches mastery level\n\n### Turning 6: BREAK (N26-N30)\n\n**Theme:** Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Status:** [RESERVED - Final confrontation and resolution]\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Counter-Spark identity revealed\n- Final confrontation and resolution\n- Complete cosmological truths revealed\n- Saga conclusion\n\n---\n\n## APPENDICES\n\n### Appendix A: Beat Method Examples\n\n**BEAT_01: Observation → Pattern Recognition**\n- **Structure:** Establish baseline POV → Notice anomaly → Recognize pattern\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low intensity\n- **Example:** Chapter 1-2 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Establish character expertise and introduce conflict\n\n**BEAT_02: Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n- **Structure:** Attempt solution → Fail → Try again with higher stakes → Succeed with cost\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium to High\n- **Example:** Chapter 4 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Demonstrate learning and cost accumulation\n\n**BEAT_03: Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution**\n- **Structure:** Immediate crisis → Escalating problems → Narrow escape with cost\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

        "id": "2849c004-e6ef-4f4a-94d0-6e22742695c5",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# compute hashes for outer vs inner duplicates\ndef sha12(p):\n    h=hashlib.sha256()\n    with open(p,'rb') as f:\n        h.update(f.read())\n    return h.hexdigest()\nouter = { 'INDEX.txt': Path(extract_dir)/'INDEX.txt',\n          'README.md': Path(extract_dir)/'README.md',\n          'QUICK-REFERENCE-CARD.md': Path(extract_dir)/'QUICK-REFERENCE-CARD.md',\n          'FILE_MANIFEST.txt': Path(extract_dir)/'FILE_MANIFEST.txt',\n          '12_CHAPTER_STRUCTURE_UPDATE_COMPLETE.md': Path(extract_dir)/'12_CHAPTER_STRUCTURE_UPDATE_COMPLETE.md'}\ninner = { name: inner_base/name for name in outer.keys() if (inner_base/name).exists()}\ncomparisons={}\nfor name, op in outer.items():\n    ip=inner.get(name)\n    if ip:\n        comparisons[name]=(sha12(op)==sha12(ip), sha12(op)[:12], sha12(ip)[:12], op.stat().st_size, ip.stat().st_size)\ncomparisons\n",
            "end_time": 1765724610.4969585,
            "final_expression_output": "{'INDEX.txt': (True, 'ca70b30740a0', 'ca70b30740a0', 9790, 9790),\n 'README.md': (True, 'ba105effab33', 'ba105effab33', 9079, 9079),\n 'QUICK-REFERENCE-CARD.md': (True, '92e7976f585e', '92e7976f585e', 4868, 4868),\n 'FILE_MANIFEST.txt': (True, '9fdb6de5847b', '9fdb6de5847b', 12692, 12692),\n '12_CHAPTER_STRUCTURE_UPDATE_COMPLETE.md': (True,\n  'f15444cc8931',\n  'f15444cc8931',\n  9144,\n  9144)}",

    "2a5d67f2-459d-4359-abab-7ff8b154bbf7": {
      "children": [
        "f8ca5928-cf28-4d53-ac69-f92106bf3559"
      ],
      "id": "2a5d67f2-459d-4359-abab-7ff8b154bbf7",

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# STORY BIBLE GENERATION TEMPLATE\n## OmniMeta Saga: Mechanical Production System for N06-N30\n\n**Version:** 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)  \n**Last Updated:** December 2025  \n**Purpose:** Formalized template structure for automated, constraint-compliant bible generation\n\n---\n\n## TEMPLATE OVERVIEW\n\nThis template defines the **14 mandatory sections** for every OmniMeta novella bible. Each section is classified by update type:\n\n**Novella Structure (12-Chapter Standard):**\n- **12 numbered chapters** (narrative spine, 1,200-2,200 words each)\n- **2 interludes** (god-vessel POV, placed between chapters)\n- **1 epilogue** (Auditor observation)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments**\n\nEach section is classified by update type:\n\n- **[FIXED]** = Never changes across novellas (locked canon)\n- **[VARIABLE]** = Unique per novella (generated fresh each time)\n- **[CUMULATIVE]** = Builds on previous novellas (growing context)\n- **[CHECKPOINT]** = Quality assurance verification gate\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS [VARIABLE]\n\n### Required Fields\n\n```\n- Novella ID: [N##]\n- Working Title: [Title - First letter locked to acrostic]\n- Turning: [#] â€“ [METAPHOR WORD] (Metaphor description)\n- Acrostic Letter: [Letter] in [WORD]\n- Acrostic Position: [#/5] in Turning [#]\n- Word Count Target: ~18,000-21,000 (12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue)\n- Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- Timeline Covered: Tide [##] through Tide [##]\n- Saga Position: Novella [##] of 30\n- Knowledge Frame: [INNOCENT/GUILTY]\n```\n\n### Bible Version History\n\n```\n- v1.0-DRAFT: [Date] - Initial generation\n- v1.1-VERIFIED: [Date] - Passed all checkpoints\n- v2.0-COMPLETE: [Date] - Approved for prose generation\n- v2.1-LOCKED: [Date] - Prose complete, archival version\n```\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint âœ“\n\n**Verification Required:**\n- [ ] Title first letter matches turning acrostic position\n- [ ] Turning metaphor correctly identified\n- [ ] Position in saga correctly numbered\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUCTURE CONTEXT [FIXED + VARIABLE]\n\n### Fixed Elements (All Novellas)\n\n**Saga Architecture:**\n- 30 novellas across 6 turnings\n- Hidden acrostic: GLASS-CRACK-FORGE-SMELT-CLEAR-BREAK\n- Dual-canon system (innocent/guilty frames)\n- God-vessel revelation protocol\n- Cost accumulation model\n- Knowledge gate progression\n\n### Variable Elements (This Novella)\n\n**Turning Context:**\n```\n- Current Turning: [#/6]\n- Turning Theme: [Metaphor + emotional arc]\n- Place in Turning: [#/5]\n- Completed Turnings: [List]\n- Remaining Turnings: [List]\n```\n\n**Narrative Position:**\n```\n- Protagonist State: [Jhace's power/cost/knowledge level]\n- Relationship State: [Jhace-Tiffani status]\n- World State: [Gods awakening %, major events]\n- Faction State: [Power balance summary]\n```\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 2: PRIOR NOVELLAS SUMMARY [CUMULATIVE]\n\n### Format: Chronological Build\n\n**For each completed novella (N01 through N[X-1]):**\n\n```\n### N## - \"[Title]\"\n**Turning:** [#] - [METAPHOR]\n**Key Events:**\n- [3-5 bullet points of major plot developments]\n\n**Character Progressions:**\n- Jhace: [Cost accumulation, power gains, psychological state]\n- Tiffani: [Cost accumulation, power gains, psychological state]\n- [Other major characters as relevant]\n\n**Relationship Milestone:**\n- [Jhace-Tiffani relationship beat]\n\n**Knowledge Revealed:**\n- [New canon information characters learned]\n\n**World Changes:**\n- [Faction shifts, god awakening, structural events]\n\n**Beat Methods Used:**\n- [List of 15 chapter beat methods + 2 interlude types]\n```\n\n### Continuity Verification Checkpoint âœ“\n\n**Verification Required:**\n- [ ] All N01-N[X-1] novellas summarized\n- [ ] No contradictions with established canon\n- [ ] Character cost levels accurately tracked\n- [ ] Beat usage patterns correctly catalogued\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 3: KNOWLEDGE-GATE COMPLIANCE [VARIABLE]\n\n### Authoritative Canon References\n\n**This section integrates with three core canon documents:**\n\n1. **`Innocent_Frame_Canon.md`** - All permissible knowledge for N01-N29\n2. **`Guilty_Frame_Canon.md`** - N30-only truths (NEVER reference in N01-N29)\n3. **`Knowledge_Gate_Progression.md`** - Timeline of when each fact unlocks\n\n**For N01-N29 Bibles:**\n- Reference ONLY `Innocent_Frame_Canon.md` and `Knowledge_Gate_Progression.md`\n- NEVER access `Guilty_Frame_Canon.md`\n- Cross-check this novella's position in progression timeline\n\n**For N30 Bible:**\n- Reference ALL three documents\n- `Guilty_Frame_Canon.md` becomes primary authority\n- No knowledge restrictions apply\n\n### Innocent Frame Protocol\n\n**This section defines what IS and IS NOT knowable as of this novella.**\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765726918.960696,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "def red_flags(text):\n    flags=[]\n    if \"â\" in text or \"�\" in text:\n        flags.append(\"encoding artifacts (â/�)\")\n    if re.search(r\"\\b1-15\\b|\\b15 numbered chapters\\b|17-chapter\", text, re.I):\n        # might be outdated\n        flags.append(\"mentions old chapter counts (15/17)\")\n    if \"[Current Date]\" in text or \"Updated: [Current Date]\" in text or \"Created: [Current Date]\" in text:\n        flags.append(\"placeholder dates\")\n    if \"CONFLUENCE CHRONICLES\" in text:\n        flags.append(\"legacy saga name\")\n    if \"MUST READ FIRST\" in text and \"v1.1\" in text and \"v1_2\" in text:\n        flags.append(\"version mismatch\")\n    return flags\n\ntar_summ=[]\nfor name,text in tar_texts.items():\n    tar_summ.append({\n        \"file\": name.split(\"/\",1)[1],\n        \"heading\": get_top_heading(text) or \"\",\n        \"purpose\": (get_purpose_line(text) or \"\").replace(\"**Purpose:**\",\"Purpose:\").replace(\"**Purpose**:\",\"Purpose:\"),\n        \"flags\": \"; \".join(red_flags(text)) or \"\"\n    })\ntar_df=pd.DataFrame(tar_summ).sort_values(\"file\")\ntar_df.head(12)\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765726939.541484,
