  \n[L520]         .footer-meta {\n[L521]             font-size: 0.9rem;\n[L522]             color: var(--copper-patina);\n[L523]             font-family: var(--font-code);\n[L524]         }\n[L525]         \n[L526]         /* Responsive Design */\n[L527]         @media (max-width: 768px) {\n[L528]             .nav-content {\n[L529]                 flex-direction: column;\n[L530]                 gap: var(--spacing-sm);\n[L531]             }\n[L532]             \n[L533]             .nav-menu {\n[L534]                 gap: var(--spacing-sm);\n[L535]             }\n[L536]             \n[L537]             .hero-title {\n[L538]                 font-size: 2.5rem;\n[L539]             }\n[L540]             \n[L541]             .main-container {\n[L542]                 padding: var(--spacing-md);\n[L543]             }\n[L544]             \n[L545]             .character-grid {\n[L546]                 grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n[L547]             }\n[L548]             \n[L549]             .magic-system {\n[L550]                 grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n[L551]             }\n[L552]             \n[L553]             .timeline-item:nth-child(odd) .timeline-content,\n[L554]             .timeline-item:nth-child(even) .timeline-content {\n[L555]                 margin: 0;\n[L556]                 padding: var(--spacing-md);\n[L557]                 text-align: left;\n[L558]             }\n[L559]             \n[L560]             .timeline-line {\n[L561]                 left: var(--spacing-md);\n[L562]             }\n[L563]             \n[L564]             .timeline-marker {\n[L565]                 left: var(--spacing-md);\n[L566]             }\n[L567]         }\n[L568]         \n[L569]         /* Scroll Animations */\n[L570]         .fade-in {\n[L571]             opacity: 0;\n[L572]             transform: translateY(30px);\n[L573]             transition: var(--transition-medium);\n[L574]         }\n[L575]         \n[L576]         .fade-in.visible {\n[L577]             opacity: 1;\n[L578]             transform: translateY(0);\n[L579]         }\n[L580]         \n[L581]         /* Loading Animation */\n[L582]         .loading {\n[L583]             display: flex;\n[L584]             justify-content: center;\n[L585]             align-items: center;\n[L586]             height: 100vh;\n[L587]             font-family: var(--font-display);\n[L588]             font-size: 1.5rem;\n[L589]             color: var(--molten-gold);\n[L590]         }\n[L591]         \n[L592]         .loading::after {\n[L593]             content: '';\n[L594]             width: 20px;\n[L595]             height: 20px;\n[L596]             border: 2px solid var(--copper-patina);\n[L597]             border-top: 2px solid var(--molten-gold);\n[L598]             border-radius: 50%;\n[L599]             animation: spin 1s linear infinite;\n[L600]             margin-left: var(--spacing-sm);\n[L601]         }\n[L602]         \n[L603]         @keyframes spin {\n[L604]             0% { transform: rotate(0deg); }\n[L605]             100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }\n[L606]         }\n[L607]         \n[L608]         /* Utility Classes */\n[L609]         .text-center { text-align: center; }\n[L610]         .text-gold { color: var(--molten-gold); }\n[L611]         .text-copper { color: var(--copper-patina); }\n[L612]         .text-violet { color: var(--form-violet); }\n[L613]         .text-amber { color: var(--wholeness-amber); }\n[L614]         .text-silver { color: var(--purity-silver); }\n[L615]         .text-red { color: var(--entropy-red); }\n[L616]         \n[L617]         .bg-dark { background: rgba(26, 26, 26, 0.8); }\n[L618]         .bg-medium { background: rgba(45, 45, 45, 0.6); }\n[L619]         .bg-light { background: rgba(74, 74, 74, 0.4); }\n[L620]         \n[L621]         .font-display { font-family: var(--font-display); }\n[L622]         .font-body { font-family: var(--font-body); }\n[L623]         .font-code { font-family: var(--font-code); }\n[L624]         .font-handwriting { font-family: var(--font-handwriting); }\n[L625]     </style>\n[L626] </head>\n[L627] <body>\n[L628]     <!-- Loading Screen -->\n[L629]     <div id=\"loading\" class=\"loading\">\n[L630]         Forging the Narrative\n[L631]     </div>\n[L632]     \n[L633]     <!-- Background Animation -->\n[L634]     <div id=\"vanta-bg\"></div>\n[L635]     \n[L636]     <!-- Navigation -->\n[L637]     <nav class=\"nav-container\">\n[L638]         <div class=\"nav-content\">\n[L639]             <div class=\"nav-title\">\n[L640]                 <i class=\"fas fa-book-open\"></i> OmniMeta Saga\n[L641]             </div>\n[L642]             <ul class=\"nav-menu\">\n[L643]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#overview\">Overview</a></li>\n[L644]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#characters\">Characters</a></li>\n[L645]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#world\">World</a></li>\n[L646]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#magic\">Magic</a></li>\n[L647]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#timeline\">Timeline</a></li>\n[L648]                 <li class=\"nav-item\"><a href=\"#themes\">Themes</a></li>\n[L649]             </ul>\n[L650]         </div>\n[L651]     </nav>\n[L652]     \n[L653]     <!-- Main Content -->\n[L654]     <main class=\"main-container\">\n[L655]         <!-- Hero Section -->\n[L656]         <section class=\"hero-section fade-in\">\n[L657]             <h1 class=\"hero-title\">Super Exponential Story Bible</h1>\n[L658]             <p class=\"hero-subtitle\">Complete Narrative Universe Documentation</p>\n[L659]             <p class=\"hero-description\">\n[L660]                 A meticulously crafted 30-novella epic fantasy spanning six \"Turnings,\" \n[L661]                 following the evolution of Jhace Torrins from craftsman to god-slayer \n[L662]                 across approximately 6 years of his life. Every element serves the \n[L663]                 central themes of partnership, cost, and balance in a world where \n[L664]                 power always extracts its price.\n[L665]             </p>\n[L666]         </section>\n[L667]         \n[L668]         <!-- Saga Overview -->\n[L669]         <section id=\"overview\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L670]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">Saga Overview</h2>\n[L671]             \n[L672]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L673]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">The Hidden Message</h3>\n[L674]                 <p>The 30 novella titles spell out a dual craft metaphor that mirrors the protagonist's spiritual journey:</p>\n[L675]                 <div class=\"magic-system\">\n[L676]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"glass\">\n[L677]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-wine-glass\"></i></div>\n[L678]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">GLASS</h4>\n[L679]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Fragile formation, transparent but breakable</p>\n[L680]                     </div>\n[L681]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"crack\">\n[L682]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-hammer\"></i></div>\n[L683]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">CRACK</h4>\n[L684]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing</p>\n[L685]                     </div>\n[L686]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"forge\">\n[L687]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-fire\"></i></div>\n[L688]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">FORGE</h4>\n[L689]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation</p>\n[L690]                     </div>\n[L691]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"smelt\">\n[L692]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-industry\"></i></div>\n[L693]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">SMELT</h4>\n[L694]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Melting down, purification, separation</p>\n[L695]                     </div>\n[L696]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"clear\">\n[L697]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-eye\"></i></div>\n[L698]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">CLEAR</h4>\n[L699]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible</p>\n[L700]                     </div>\n[L701]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"break\">\n[L702]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-bolt\"></i></div>\n[L703]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">BREAK</h4>\n[L704]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">Final shattering, ultimate transformation</p>\n[L705]                     </div>\n[L706]                 </div>\n[L707]             </div>\n[L708]             \n[L709]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L710]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Core Premise</h3>\n[L711]                 <p>A mysterious projectile pierces completely through the planet Te'Oga, creating a permanent hole and fundamentally breaking planetary physics. This \"Piercing\" event awakens a dormant force called <strong>Soulpulse</strong> that did NOT exist before.</p>\n[L712]                 \n[L713]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L714]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L715]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">Physical Consequences</span>\n[L716]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L717]                     </div>\n[L718]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L719]                         <ul>\n[L720]                             <li>Axial tilt snapped to ~0° (no meaningful seasons)</li>\n[L721]                             <li>Catastrophic quakes and tsunamis reshape continents</li>\n[L722]                             <li>Permanent climate band disruption</li>\n[L723]                             <li>\"Scar storms\" near entry/exit wounds</li>\n[L724]                             <li>Resonance hotspots along damage path</li>\n[L725]                         </ul>\n[L726]                     </div>\n[L727]                 </div>\n[L728]                 \n[L729]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L730]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L731]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">Metaphysical Consequences</span>\n[L732]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L733]                     </div>\n[L734]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L735]                         <ul>\n[L736]                             <li><strong>Soulpulse/Resonance emerges for the first time</strong></li>\n[L737]                             <li>Conscious will can now affect matter</li>\n[L738]                             <li>Four ancient gods begin awakening in mortal forms</li>\n[L739]                             <li>The Auditor begins orchestrating events toward stasis</li>\n[L740]                         </ul>\n[L741]                     </div>\n[L742]                 </div>\n[L743]             </div>\n[L744]         </section>\n[L745]         \n[L746]         <!-- Characters Section -->\n[L747]         <section id=\"characters\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L748]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">Character Profiles</h2>\n[L749]             \n[L750]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L751]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Protagonists</h3>\n[L752]                 <div class=\"character-grid\">\n[L753]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L754]                         <h4 class=\"character-name\">Jhace Torrins</h4>\n[L755]                         <p class=\"character-role\">The Craftsman Savior</p>\n[L756]                         <div class=\"character-description\">\n[L757]                             <strong>Age (N01):</strong> 28 turns<br>\n[L758]                             <strong>Craft:</strong> Forge-smith (gridwright), infrastructure specialist<br>\n[L759]                             <strong>Psychological Wound:</strong> Survivor's guilt - parents died in structural collapse at age 7, carries 23 names of the dead<br>\n[L760]                             <strong>Core Drive:</strong> Obsessive need to save everyone, prevent all deaths through vigilance<br>\n[L761]                             <strong>Soulpulse Alignment:</strong> Primarily FORM (holding, stabilizing, structural integrity)<br>\n[L762]                             <strong>Character Arc:</strong> Isolated savior → Collaborative leader → God-confronter\n[L763]                         </div>\n[L764]                     </div>\n[L765]                     \n[L766]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L767]                         <h4 class=\"character-name\">Tiffani Merrow</h4>\n[L768]                         <p class=\"character-role\">The Perfectionist</p>\n[L769]                         <div class=\"character-description\">\n[L770]                             <strong>Age (N01):</strong> 26 turns<br>\n[L771]                             <strong>Craft:</strong> Glassworker, precision measurement and structural analysis<br>\n[L772]                             <strong>Psychological Wound:</strong> Guilt over friend Marisse's death in glasshouse accident (miscalculation)<br>\n[L773]                             <strong>Core Drive:</strong> Perfectionism, belief that vigilance alone prevents catastrophe<br>\n[L774]                             <strong>Soulpulse Alignment:</strong> Primarily WHOLENESS (mending, connecting, restoring)<br>\n[L775]                             <strong>Ultimate Fate:</strong> Dies in N05 from Soulpulse burnout holding catastrophic structural failure\n[L776]                         </div>\n[L777]                     </div>\n[L778]                 </div>\n[L779]             </div>\n[L780]             \n[L781]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L782]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">The Four God-Vessels</h3>\n[L783]                 <div class=\"magic-system\">\n[L784]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"form\">\n[L785]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-cube\"></i></div>\n[L786]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">Corlexi (Xilcore)</h4>\n[L787]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">FORM - Hold, stabilize, impose structure</p>\n[L788]                         <p>God of structure, order, frameworks. Cost: Muscle ache, joint stiffness, rigid tension. Danger: Over-stabilization, brittleness.</p>\n[L789]                     </div>\n[L790]                     \n[L791]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"wholeness\">\n[L792]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-link\"></i></div>\n[L793]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">Salee (Leesa)</h4>\n[L794]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">WHOLENESS - Mend, connect, restore continuity</p>\n[L795]                         <p>God of connection, unity, healing. Cost: Empathy overload, emotional drain. Danger: Boundary dissolution, compassion paralysis.</p>\n[L796]                     </div>\n[L797]                     \n[L798]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"purity\">\n[L799]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-gem\"></i></div>\n[L800]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">Mobel (Blemo)</h4>\n[L801]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">PURITY - Refine, distill, isolate essence</p>\n[L802]                         <p>God of perfection, separation, righteousness. Cost: Migraine, hyper-focus burnout. Danger: Obsessive perfectionism, isolation.</p>\n[L803]                     </div>\n[L804]                     \n[L805]                     <div class=\"principle-card\" data-principle=\"entropy\">\n[L806]                         <div class=\"principle-icon\"><i class=\"fas fa-skull\"></i></div>\n[L807]                         <h4 class=\"principle-name\">Eries (Seeri)</h4>\n[L808]                         <p class=\"principle-verb\">ENTROPY - Undo, release, accelerate decay</p>\n[L809]                         <p>God of endings, release, natural decay. Cost: Numbness, apathy, dissociation. Danger: Nihilism, destructive spirals.</p>\n[L810]                     </div>\n[L811]                 </div>\n[L812]             </div>\n[L813]             \n[L814]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L815]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">The Antagonist</h3>\n[L816]                 <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L817]                     <h4 class=\"character-name\">The Auditor / Counter-Spark</h4>\n[L818]                     <p class=\"character-role\">The Hidden Orchestrator</p>\n[L819]                     <div class=\"character-description\">\n[L820]                         <strong>True Identity:</strong> Counter-Spark (Stasis principle) in mortal form<br>\n[L821]                         <strong>Narrative Role:</strong> Unreliable narrator across all interludes and epilogues<br>\n[L822]                         <strong>Voice:</strong> Profane, sardonic, knowing, contemptuous<br>\n[L823]                         <strong>Facade:</strong> Appears supportive of god awakening, documents events as neutral observer<br>\n[L824]                         <strong>True Goal:</strong> Shepherding gods toward full power TO ELIMINATE THEM PERMANENTLY<br>\n[L825]                         <strong>Revelation Timing:</strong> Turning 6 (N26-N30)<br>\n[L826]                         <strong>Dramatic Irony:</strong> Every \"observation\" is hostile manipulation\n[L827]                     </div>\n[L828]                 </div>\n[L829]             </div>\n[L830]         </section>\n[L831]         \n[L832]         <!-- World Section -->\n[L833]         <section id=\"world\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L834]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">World & Setting</h2>\n[L835]             \n[L836]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L837]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Planet Te'Oga</h3>\n[L838]                 <p>A mysterious projectile has pierced completely through the planet, creating a permanent hole and fundamentally altering its physics and climate.</p>\n[L839]                 \n[L840]                 <div class=\"interactive-chart\">\n[L841]                     <h4 class=\"chart-title\">Planetary Changes</h4>\n[L842]                     <canvas id=\"planetaryChart\" width=\"400\" height=\"200\"></canvas>\n[L843]                 </div>\n[L844]             </div>\n[L845]             \n[L846]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L847]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Time & Measurement Systems</h3>\n[L848]                 <p>The world uses indigenous time and measurement units, never Earth terms.</p>\n[L849]                 \n[L850]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L851]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L852]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">Time Hierarchy</span>\n[L853]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L854]                     </div>\n[L855]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L856]                         <ul>\n[L857]                             <li><strong>Tide</strong> = ~24 hours (with phases: Kindling → Pouring → Shaping → Striking → Quench)</li>\n[L858]                             <li><strong>Anneal</strong> = ~7 tides (~1 week)</li>\n[L859]                             <li><strong>Crucible</strong> = ~30 tides (~1 month)</li>\n[L860]                             <li><strong>Turn</strong> = ~1 year (personal age)</li>\n[L861]                             <li><strong>Cast</strong> = ~1 year (historical time)</li>\n[L862]                             <li><strong>Turning</strong> = ~5-10 casts (historical era)</li>\n[L863]                         </ul>\n[L864]                     </div>\n[L865]                 </div>\n[L866]             </div>\n[L867]         </section>\n[L868]         \n[L869]         <!-- Magic System -->\n[L870]         <section id=\"magic\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L871]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">Magic System</h2>\n[L872]             \n[L873]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L874]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Soulpulse (Resonance)</h3>\n[L875]                 <p>Conscious will given physical form through manipulation of matter's fundamental resonance. Always visible as colored light corresponding to Principle.</p>\n[L876]                 \n[L877]                 <div class=\"interactive-chart\">\n[L878]                     <h4 class=\"chart-title\">Cost Progression System</h4>\n[L879]                     <canvas id=\"costChart\" width=\"400\" height=\"200\"></canvas>\n[L880]                 </div>\n[L881]             </div>\n[L882]             \n[L883]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L884]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Cost Stages</h3>\n[L885]                 <div class=\"character-grid\">\n[L886]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L887]                         <h4 class=\"character-name text-amber\">Stage 1: Strain</h4>\n[L888]                         <p class=\"character-description\">\n[L889]                             Initial fatigue, minor tremors during use, temporary effects that fade with rest.\n[L890]                         </p>\n[L891]                     </div>\n[L892]                     \n[L893]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L894]                         <h4 class=\"character-name text-copper\">Stage 2: Fatigue</h4>\n[L895]                         <p class=\"character-description\">\n[L896]                             Persistent tremor baseline, exhaustion after major use, functional impairment begins.\n[L897]                         </p>\n[L898]                     </div>\n[L899]                     \n[L900]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L901]                         <h4 class=\"character-name text-red\">Stage 3: Burnout</h4>\n[L902]                         <p class=\"character-description\">\n[L903]                             Vein-lines permanently visible, fine motor control compromised, recovery takes longer.\n[L904]                         </p>\n[L905]                     </div>\n[L906]                     \n[L907]                     <div class=\"character-card\">\n[L908]                         <h4 class=\"character-name text-violet\">Stage 4: Collapse</h4>\n[L909]                         <p class=\"character-description\">\n[L910]                             Complete breakdown, potential death from environmental factors, irreversible without intervention.\n[L911]                         </p>\n[L912]                     </div>\n[L913]                 </div>\n[L914]             </div>\n[L915]             \n[L916]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L917]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Power Evolution</h3>\n[L918]                 <div class=\"timeline-container\">\n[L919]                     <div class=\"timeline-line\"></div>\n[L920]                     \n[L921]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L922]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L923]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L924]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">N01-N05: Emergence</h4>\n[L925]                             <p class=\"timeline-period\">Basic manipulation, intuitive use, high cost, discovery of coordination</p>\n[L926]                         </div>\n[L927]                     </div>\n[L928]                     \n[L929]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L930]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L931]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L932]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">N06-N15: Training</h4>\n[L933]                             <p class=\"timeline-period\">Faction techniques, martial applications, combat magic emerges</p>\n[L934]                         </div>\n[L935]                     </div>\n[L936]                     \n[L937]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L938]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L939]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L940]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">N16-N25: Combat</h4>\n[L941]                             <p class=\"timeline-period\">Anime-style battle magic, Ki/Chakra-like systems, open divine warfare</p>\n[L942]                         </div>\n[L943]                     </div>\n[L944]                     \n[L945]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L946]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L947]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L948]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">N26-N30: God-Tier</h4>\n[L949]                             <p class=\"timeline-period\">Planetary-scale effects, god confrontations, ultimate mastery</p>\n[L950]                         </div>\n[L951]                     </div>\n[L952]                 </div>\n[L953]             </div>\n[L954]         </section>\n[L955]         \n[L956]         <!-- Timeline Section -->\n[L957]         <section id=\"timeline\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L958]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">Saga Timeline</h2>\n[L959]             \n[L960]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L961]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">The Six Turnings</h3>\n[L962]                 <div class=\"timeline-container\">\n[L963]                     <div class=\"timeline-line\"></div>\n[L964]                     \n[L965]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L966]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L967]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L968]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">TURNING 1: of forces, fracture lines</p>\n[L979]                             <p>Gods 30-40% conscious, faction formation, fragmentary divine memories, political pressure mounts</p>\n[L980] class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L985]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L986]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">TURNING 3: FORGE</h4>\n[L987] divine warfare begins</p>\n[L989]                         </div>\n[L990]                     </div>\n[L991]                     \n[L992]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L993]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L994]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L995]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">TURNING 4: <p>Lattice system developed, managed opposition, compromise emerging, balance framework tested</p>\n[L1007]                         </div>\n[L1008]                     </div>\n[L1009]                     \n[L1010]                     <div class=\"timeline-item\">\n[L1011]                         <div class=\"timeline-marker\"></div>\n[L1012]                         <div class=\"timeline-content\">\n[L1013]                             <h4 class=\"timeline-title\">TURNING 6: BREAK</h4>\n[L1014]                             <p class=\"timeline-period\">N26-N30 • Final shattering, ultimate transformation</p>\n[L1015]                             <p>[RESERVED] Final confrontation and resolution, complete revelation</p>\n[L1016]                         </div>\n[L1017]                     </div>\n[L1018]                 </div>\n[L1019]             </div>\n[L1020]         </section>\n[L1021]         \n[L1022]         <!-- Themes Section -->\n[L1023]         <section id=\"themes\" class=\"section fade-in\">\n[L1024]             <h2 class=\"section-title\">Themes & Story Arcs</h2>\n[L1025]             \n[L1026]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L1027]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Core Themes</h3>\n[L1028]                 \n[L1029]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1030]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1031]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">1. Craft as Metaphor</span>\n[L1032]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1033]                     </div>\n[L1034]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1035]                         <p><strong>Glass-Making (Turning 1):</strong> Fragile formation, transparent but breakable. Tiffani's craft mirrors her perfectionism and vulnerability.</p>\n[L1036]                         <p><strong>Metal-Forging (Turning 3):</strong> Hammering into shape through intense heat. Jhace's craft mirrors his approach to problems.</p>\n[L1037]                         <p><strong>Dual Craft Journey:</strong> Both crafts require understanding stress, heat, and transformation. Both become tools of resistance against divine chaos.</p>\n[L1038]                     </div>\n[L1039]                 </div>\n[L1040]                 \n[L1041]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1042]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1043]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">2. Partnership vs. Isolation</span>\n[L1044]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1045]                     </div>\n[L1046]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1047]                         <p><strong>Jhace's Isolation:</strong> Carries 23 names of dead from childhood trauma. Believes he must save everyone alone.</p>\n[L1048]                         <p><strong>Tiffani's Isolation:</strong> Guilt over Marisse's death drives perfectionism. Works alone to prevent mistakes.</p>\n[L1049]                         <p><strong>Load-Bearing System:</strong> They become structural support for each other. \"Remove one, both collapse.\"</p>\n[L1050]                         <p><strong>Third Path:</strong> Neither institutional control (RRO) nor divine chaos (gods). Human collaboration as alternative.</p>\n[L1051]                     </div>\n[L1052]                 </div>\n[L1053]                 \n[L1054]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1055]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1056]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">3. Cost of Power</span>\n[L1057]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1058]                     </div>\n[L1059]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1060]                         <p><strong>Visible Cost:</strong> Every Soulpulse use creates physical markers - tremors, vein-lines, exhaustion.</p>\n[L1061]                         <p><strong>Exponential Cost:</strong> Each use costs more than the last. Users must learn efficiency or be destroyed.</p>\n[L1062]                         <p><strong>Tiffani's Death:</strong> Ultimate cost - burnout from holding impossible structure. Her death saves others but destroys her.</p>\n[L1063]                         <p><strong>Jhace's Survival:</strong> Must learn to bear cost without being consumed. Tiffani's death teaches him limits.</p>\n[L1064]                     </div>\n[L1065]                 </div>\n[L1066]                 \n[L1067]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1068]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1069]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">4. Order vs. Chaos</span>\n[L1070]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1071]                     </div>\n[L1072]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1073]                         <p><strong>Form + Purity (Order):</strong> Structure, separation, judgment, perfection. Corlexi and Mobel represent institutional approach.</p>\n[L1074]                         <p><strong>Wholeness + Entropy (Chaos):</strong> Connection, dissolution, unity, release. Salee and Eries represent boundary dissolution.</p>\n[L1075]                         <p><strong>Jhace + Tiffani Balance:</strong> Form + Wholeness = Structural healing. Neither pure order nor pure chaos.</p>\n[L1076]                         <p><strong>Divine Warfare:</strong> Four principles in cosmic conflict. Each god-vessel believes their principle is correct.</p>\n[L1077]                     </div>\n[L1078]                 </div>\n[L1079]                 \n[L1080]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1081]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1082]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">5. Observation vs. Action</span>\n[L1083]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1084]                     </div>\n[L1085]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1086]                         <p><strong>Tiffani's Approach:</strong> Measure first, understand patterns, act with precision. \"Stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks.\"</p>\n[L1087]                         <p><strong>Jhace's Approach:</strong> Act first, hold the structure, measure consequences later. \"Sometimes you have to choose who dies.\"</p>\n[L1088]                         <p><strong>Auditor's Approach:</strong> Observe only, never intervene. False facade - actually orchestrating events.</p>\n[L1089]                         <p><strong>Synthesis:</strong> Both observation AND action needed. Neither approach sufficient alone. Partnership succeeds because they balance each other.</p>\n[L1090]                     </div>\n[L1091]                 </div>\n[L1092]             </div>\n[L1093]             \n[L1094]             <div class=\"subsection\">\n[L1095]                 <h3 class=\"subsection-title\">Major Story Arcs</h3>\n[L1096]                 \n[L1097]                 <div class=\"collapsible\">\n[L1098]                     <div class=\"collapsible-header\">\n[L1099]                         <span class=\"collapsible-title\">Jhace & Tiffani Relationship Arc</span>\n[L1100]                         <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-down collapsible-icon\"></i>\n[L1101]                     </div>\n[L1102]                     <div class=\"collapsible-content\">\n[L1103]                         <p><strong>N01: The Grip That Holds</strong> - From parallel observation through competence recognition to partnership formation and commitment.</p>\n[L1104]                         <p><strong>N02-N04: Partnership Deepens</strong> - Interdependence solidifies, political pressure mounts, load-bearing system established.</p>\n[L1105]                         <p><strong>N05: Sight of Dying Light</strong> - Tiffani's death triggers Jhace's Fathom Break, breaking final seal on gods.</p>\n[L1106] \n[L1107] The file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n[L1108] "
          ]
        },

    "348e9511-3130-4c7d-a30f-bad3e089f077": {
      "children": [
        "6f4ff07b-7c1f-408a-b536-f213e1bace5b"
      ],
      "id": "348e9511-3130-4c7d-a30f-bad3e089f077",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "m your uploads)\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 WHAT CHANGED\n\n### OLD STRUCTURE (Pre-December 2025):\n```\n- 15 numbered chapters\n- 800-1,300 words per chapter\n- 2 interludes (800-1,300 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,300 words)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n```\n\n### NEW STRUCTURE (December 2025 Forward):\n```\n- 12 numbered chapters\n- 1,200-2,200 words per chapter (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- = 15 total narrative segments (maintains original vision)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ WHAT'S BEEN UPDATED\n\n### Chapter Counts\n- ✅ All references to \"15 chapters\" → \"12 chapters\"\n- ✅ All references to \"fifteen chapters\" → \"twelve chapters\"\n- ✅ Chapter counting logic updated throughout\n\n### Word Counts\n- ✅ Chapter range: 800-1,300 → 1,200-2,200 words\n- ✅ Interlude range: 800-1,300 → 600-900 words\n- ✅ Epilogue range: 800-1,300 → 800-1,000 words\n\n### Tier Balance (for 12 chapters)\n- ✅ Tier 1 (High Impact): 4-5 chapters (was 5-6)\n- ✅ Tier 2 (Medium Impact): 3-4 chapters (unchanged)\n- ✅ Tier 3 (Low Impact): 3-4 chapters (was 4-6)\n\n### Emotional Intensity Distribution (for 12 chapters)\n- ✅ HIGH: 3-4 chapters per novella (was 3-5)\n- ✅ MEDIUM: 5-6 chapters per novella (was 5-7)\n- ✅ LOW: 2-3 chapters per novella (was 3-5)\n\n### Special Cases\n- ✅ N04-N05 17-chapter exception marked as OBSOLETE\n- ✅ All references updated to note December 2025 structural change\n- ✅ Version numbers bumped where appropriate (v1.1 → v1.2)\n\n---\n\n## 🚨 CRITICAL NOTES\n\n### Files NOT Included (Require Regeneration)\nThese files exist in your project but were NOT updated because they require full regeneration rather than patching:\n\n1. **N01-Bible-REWRITTEN.md** - Needs full regeneration for 12 chapters\n2. **N02-Bible-REWRITTEN.md** - Needs full regeneration for 12 chapters\n3. **N03-Bible-COMPLETE.md** - Needs full regeneration for 12 chapters\n4. **N04-Bible-COMPLETE.md** - Needs full regeneration for 12 chapters\n5. **N05-Bible-COMPLETE.md** - \n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765724585.6127949,
        "end_turn": null,

                  "text": "m your uploads)\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 WHAT CHANGED\n\n### OLD STRUCTURE (Pre-December 2025):\n```\n- 15 numbered chapters\n- 800-1,300 words per chapter\n- 2 interludes (800-1,300 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,300 words)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n```\n\n### NEW STRUCTURE (December 2025 Forward):\n```\n- 12 numbered chapters\n- 1,200-2,200 words per chapter (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- = 15 total narrative segments (maintains original vision)\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words\n```\n\n---\n\n## ✅ WHAT'S BEEN UPDATED\n\n### Chapter Counts\n- ✅ All references to \"15 chapters\" → \"12 chapters\"\n- ✅ All references to \"fifteen chapters\" → \"twelve chapters\"\n- ✅ Chapter counting logic updated throughout\n\n### Word Counts\n- ✅ Chapter range: 800-1,300 → 1,200-2,200 words\n- ✅ Interlude range: 800-1,300 → 600-900 words\n- ✅ Epilogue range: 800-1,300 → 800-1,000 words\n\n### Tier Balance (for 12 chapters)\n- ✅ Tier 1 (High Impact): 4-5 chapters (was 5-6)\n- ✅ Tier 2 (Medium Impact): 3-4 chapters (unchanged)\n- ✅ Tier 3 (Low Impact): 3-4 chapters (was 4-6)\n\n### Emotional Intensity Distribution (for 12 chapters)\n- ✅ HIGH: 3-4 chapters per novella (was 3-5)\n- ✅ MEDIUM: 5-6 chapters per novella (was 5-7)\n- ✅ LOW: 2-3 chapters per novella (was 3-5)\n\n### Special Cases\n- ✅ N04-N05 17-chapter exception marked as OBSOLETE\n- ✅ All references updated to note December 2025 structural change\n- ✅ Version numbers bumped where appropriate (v1.1 → v1.2)\n\n---\n\n## 🚨 CRITICAL NOTES\n\n### Files NOT Included (Require Regeneration)\nThese files exist in your project but were NOT updated because they require full regeneration rather than patching:\n\n1. **N01-Bible-REWRITTEN.md** -           "text": "overs political complications |\n| 7 | \"Misuse\" | Observation â†’ Pattern Recognition | 10 | 2 | Low | Jhace recognizes misuse patterns |\n| 8 | \"Inquiry Board\" | Misdirection Plant â†’ False Certainty | 8 | 1 | Medium | Sets up false resolution |\n| 9 | \"Learning Curve\" | Teaching Moment â†’ Student Failure â†’ Lesson | 13 | 2 | Low | Jhace teaches Chloen |\n| 10 | \"Cracks in Trust\" | Relationship Shift â†’ Power Dynamic Change | 12 | 1 | High | Major J-T conflict and resolution |\n| 11 | \"Near Collapse\" | External Threat â†’ Narrow Escape | 3 | 1 | High | Jhace nearly breaks himself |\n| 12 | \"Holding Together\" | Intimacy â†’ Vulnerability â†’ Connection | 17 | 1 | Medium | Share trauma, deepen bond |\n| 13 | \"The Grip\" (Action) | Battle â†’ Survival â†’ Cost | 23 | 1 | High | Major action climax |\n| 14 | \"Consequences\" | Break-Down (Post-Crisis) | 7 | 2 | Medium | Process aftermath of Ch13 |\n| 15 | \"Choosing the Weight\" | Rest â†’ Reflection â†’ Renewed Purpose | 21 | 2 | Low | Closing, commitment scene |\n\n### Interlude Analysis\n\n| Position | Title | Type | POV | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| After Ch 5 | \"Edges and Echoes\" | Build-Up | Mobel (Auditor-narrated) | First god-vessel observation |\n| After Ch 10 | \"Hinges\" | Build-Up | Corlexi (Auditor-narrated) | Second god-vessel observation |\n\n### Beat Statistics\n\n**Total Unique Beats Used:** 13 different beat methods  \n**Tier Distribution:**\n- Tier 1 (High Impact): 7 uses (Beats: 11, 1, 8, 12, 3, 17, 23)\n- Tier 2 (Medium Impact): 8 uses (Beats: 6, 7, 16, 10, 13, 7, 21)\n- Tier 3 (Low Impact): 0 uses\n\n**Repeated Beats:**\n- Beat 6 (Build-Up): Ch 1, Ch 2 - Ch14â†’Ch15)\n\n### Beat Statistics\n\n**Tier Distribution:**\n\n==== N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md ====\n# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n---\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External Threat → Narrow Escape |\n| **BEAT_04** | Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | **Beat 16** | Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)* |\n| **BEAT_05** | Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe | **Beat 13** | Teaching Moment → Student Failure → Lesson Learned *(or Beat 20 if student-success weighted)* |\n| **BEAT_06** | Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate |\n| **BEAT_07** | Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate *(institutional confrontation analog)* |\n| **BEAT_08** | Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment | **Beat 17** | Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)* |\n\n---\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)**\n- Use when: Character investigates, discovers truth, truth creates choice/complication\n- Example: Ch 8 (Jhace resists Tiffani's data, investigates hold patterns, discovers efficiency truth)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 28 (Moral Compromise → Guilt → Justification)**\n- Use when: Offer requires ethical compromise, character feels moral weight, accepts or refuses with consequences\n- Example: If BEAT_04 chapter centers on moral pressure rather than investigation\n\n**Logging Guideline:** Default to Beat 16 unless moral compromise is central theme.\n\n---\n\n### **BEAT_08 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes emotional vulnerability, sharing trauma, relationship deepening\n- Example: Ch 11 (trauma exchange, mutual need acknowledged)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes rest, reflection, renewed commitment with minimal vulnerability\n- Example: Ch 7 (data compilation), Ch 12 (writing priorities on forge wall)\n\n**Logging Guideline:** If intimacy/vulnerability present → Beat 17. If primarily rest/reflection → Beat 21.\n\n---\n\n## N01 CHAPTER LOGGING (CURRENT STATUS)\n\n### **Locked Chapters (1-5):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 1 | Something Wrong | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 2 | Glass That Listens | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 3 | The Sky Breaks | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 4 | After the Shudder | BEAT_02 | Beat 1 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 5 | Lines That Don't Lie | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n\n**Note:** Ch 1-2 use BEAT_01 consecutively (opening exception for parallel POV establishment). Ch 5 returns to BEAT_01 for systematic observation/pattern work.\n\n---\n\n### **Remaining Chapters (6-12):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 6 | Weight That Won't Yield | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | To Write |\n| 7 | Quiet Calculations | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 | To Write |\n| 8 | Proof and Resistance | BEAT_04 | Beat 16 | To Write |\n| 9 | Ash and Claims | BEAT_07 | Beat 26 | To Write |\n| 10 | Near Collapse | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | To Write |\n| 11 | Holding Together | BEAT_08 | Beat 17 | To Write |\n| 12 | Choosing the Weight | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 | To Write |\n\n**Beat Usage Summary:**\n- BEAT_01 (Beat 10): Ch 1, 2, 5\n- BEAT_02 (Beat 1): Ch 4\n- BEAT_03 (Beat 3): Ch 3, 6, 10\n- BEAT_04 (Beat 16): Ch 8\n- BEAT_07 (Beat 26): Ch 9\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 17): Ch 11\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 21): Ch 7, 12\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID LOGGING FORMAT (REQUIRED)\n\nEach chapter entry must list both local and saga beat IDs:\n\n**Template:**\n```\n### CHAPTER X: \"[Title]\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_0X ([N01 structure name])\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat Y ([Saga structure name])\n```\n\n**Example (Ch 6):**\n```\n### CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n```\n\n---\n\n## VALIDATOR ENFORCEMENT (N01 LOCAL ONLY)\n\n**N01 Validator checks:**\n- ✅ No consecutive identical beats (BEAT_03 → BEAT_03)\n- ✅ No rotation patterns (BEAT_01 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_03 → BEAT_01...)\n- ✅ Beat-specific constraints from N01 Beat Catalog\n\n**Opening Exception:**\nN01 permits BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 only for Ch 1–2 to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror. This is the sole consecutive-beat exception in N01.\n\n**Validator does NOT check:**\n- ❌ Saga-level beat sequences (handled by cross-novella tracker)\n- ❌ Beat frequency across N01-N30 (handled by saga audit)\n\n---\n\n## SAGA-LEVEL TRACKING (CROSS-NOVELLA)\n\n**Saga Beat Audit tracks:**\n- ✅ Which Saga Beats used in which novellas\n- ✅ Forbidden follow-pairs (if Beat X → Beat Y in N01, cannot repeat in N02-N30)\n- ✅ Beat frequency across all 30 novellas\n- ✅ Minimum separation requirements for major beats (27, 29, 30)\n\n**Saga tracking uses:**\n- Beat IDs from \"Saga Beat\" column in chapter tables above\n- N01's local beat constraints don't apply to other novellas\n- Each novella can develop own local beat system mapped to saga repertoire\n\n---\n\n## WHY TWO SYSTEMS COEXIST\n\n### **N01 Local System (8 beats):**\n- **Scope:** Within N01's 12 chapters\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation inside one novella\n- **Enforcement:** N01 Validator, Constraint_Systems.md\n- **Granularity:** Fine-tuned for N01's specific narrative needs\n\n### **Saga System (30+ beats):**\n- **Scope:** Across all 30 novellas\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation across entire saga\n- **Enforcement:** Beat Sequence Audit Tracker (N01-N30)\n- **Granularity:** Broad structural categories applicable saga-wide\n\n**These operate at different scales and complement each other without conflict.**\n\n---\n\n## UPDATING THIS MAPPING\n\n**When N01 adds new local beats:**\n1. Add row to Core Mapping Table\n2. Identify closest Saga Beat analog\n3. Update chapter logging tables\n4. Add usage notes if context-dependent\n\n**When Saga adds new beats (Beat 31+):**\n1. Evaluate if any N01 local beats map more precisely to new beat\n\n==== N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md ====\n# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,240 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace inspects heat-grid junctions in his ritualistic pre-Piercing routine. He detects an \"off-note\" in the grid hum—pressure deviation signaling potential failure. Bram teases him about working alone and obsessive checking patterns. Ground tremor hits. Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 5: \"Lines That Don't Lie\"**\n"
        },

TBD] | Rest â†’ Reflection â†’ Renewed Purpose | 21 | 2 | Low | Closing reflection |\n\n### N02 Forbidden Sequences (Created)\n\n15 new sequences added to tracker (Ch1â†’Ch2 through Ch14â†’Ch15)\n\n### Beat Statistics\n\n**Tier Distribution:**\n\n==== N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md ====\n# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n---\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External Threat → Narrow Escape |\n| **BEAT_04** | Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | **Beat 16** | Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)* |\n| **BEAT_05** | Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe | **Beat 13** | Teaching Moment → Student Failure → Lesson Learned *(or Beat 20 if student-success weighted)* |\n| **BEAT_06** | Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate |\n| **BEAT_07** | Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate *(institutional confrontation analog)* |\n| **BEAT_08** | Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment | **Beat 17** | Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)* |\n\n---\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)**\n- Use when: Character investigates, discovers truth, truth creates choice/complication\n- Example: Ch 8 (Jhace resists Tiffani's data, investigates hold patterns, discovers efficiency truth)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 28 (Moral Compromise → Guilt → Justification)**\n- Use when: Offer requires ethical compromise, character feels moral weight, accepts or refuses with consequences\n- Example: If BEAT_04 chapter centers on moral pressure rather than investigation\n\n**Logging Guideline:** Default to Beat 16 unless moral compromise is central theme.\n\n---\n\n### **BEAT_08 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes emotional vulnerability, sharing trauma, relationship deepening\n- Example: Ch 11 (trauma exchange, mutual need acknowledged)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes rest, reflection, renewed commitment with minimal vulnerability\n- Example: Ch 7 (data compilation), Ch 12 (writing priorities on forge wall)\n\n**Logging Guideline:** If intimacy/vulnerability present → Beat 17. If primarily rest/reflection → Beat 21.\n\n---\n\n## N01 CHAPTER LOGGING (CURRENT STATUS)\n\n### **Locked Chapters (1-5):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 1 | Something Wrong | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 2 | Glass That Listens | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 3 | The Sky Breaks | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 4 | After the Shudder | BEAT_02 | Beat 1 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 5 | Lines That Don't Lie | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n\n**Note:** Ch 1-2 use BEAT_01 consecutively (opening exception for parallel POV establishment). Ch 5 returns to BEAT_01 for systematic observation/pattern work.\n\n---\n\n### **Remaining Chapters (6-12):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 6 | Weight That Won't Yield | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | To Write |\n| 7 | Quiet Calculations | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 | To Write |\n| 8 | Proof and Resistance | BEAT_04 | Beat 16 | To Write |\n| 9 | Ash and Claims | BEAT_07 | Beat 26 | To Write |\n| 10 | Near Collapse | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | To Write |\n| 11 | Holding Together | BEAT_08 | Beat 17 | To Write |\n| 12 | Choosing the Weight | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 | To Write |\n\n**Beat Usage Summary:**\n- BEAT_01 (Beat 10): Ch 1, 2, 5\n- BEAT_02 (Beat 1): Ch 4\n- BEAT_03 (Beat 3): Ch 3, 6, 10\n- BEAT_04 (Beat 16): Ch 8\n- BEAT_07 (Beat 26): Ch 9\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 17): Ch 11\n- BEAT_08 (Beat 21): Ch 7, 12\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID LOGGING FORMAT (REQUIRED)\n\nEach chapter entry must list both local and saga beat IDs:\n\n**Template:**\n```\n### CHAPTER X: \"[Title]\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_0X ([N01 structure name])\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat Y ([Saga structure name])\n```\n\n**Example (Ch 6):**\n```\n### CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"\n- **N01 Local Beat:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n```\n\n---\n\n## VALIDATOR ENFORCEMENT (N01 LOCAL ONLY)\n\n**N01 Validator checks:**\n- ✅ No consecutive identical beats (BEAT_03 → BEAT_03)\n- ✅ No rotation patterns (BEAT_01 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_03 → BEAT_01...)\n- ✅ Beat-specific constraints from N01 Beat Catalog\n\n**Opening Exception:**\nN01 permits BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 only for Ch 1–2 to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror. This is the sole consecutive-beat exception in N01.\n\n**Validator does NOT check:**\n- ❌ Saga-level beat sequences (handled by cross-novella tracker)\n- ❌ Beat frequency across N01-N30 (handled by saga audit)\n\n---\n\n## SAGA-LEVEL TRACKING (CROSS-NOVELLA)\n\n**Saga Beat Audit tracks:**\n- ✅ Which Saga Beats used in which novellas\n- ✅ Forbidden follow-pairs (if Beat X → Beat Y in N01, cannot repeat in N02-N30)\n- ✅ Beat frequency across all 30 novellas\n- ✅ Minimum separation requirements for major beats (27, 29, 30)\n\n**Saga tracking uses:**\n- Beat IDs from \"Saga Beat\" column in chapter tables above\n- N01's local beat constraints don't apply to other novellas\n- Each novella can develop own local beat system mapped to saga repertoire\n\n---\n\n## WHY TWO SYSTEMS COEXIST\n\n### **N01 Local System (8 beats):**\n- **Scope:** Within N01's 12 chapters\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation inside one novella\n- **Enforcement:** N01 Validator, Constraint_Systems.md\n- **Granularity:** Fine-tuned for N01's specific narrative needs\n\n### **Saga System (30+ beats):**\n- **Scope:** Across all 30 novellas\n- **Purpose:** Prevent pattern formation across entire saga\n- **Enforcement:** Beat Sequence Audit Tracker (N01-N30)\n- **Granularity:** Broad structural categories applicable saga-wide\n\n**These operate at different scales and complement each other without conflict.**\n\n---\n\n## UPDATING THIS MAPPING\n\n**When N01 adds new local beats:**\n1. Add row to Core Mapping Table\n2. Identify closest Saga Beat analog\n3. Update chapter logging tables\n4. Add usage notes if context-dependent\n\n**When Saga adds new beats (Beat 31+):**\n1. Evaluate if any N01 local beats map more precisely to new beat\n\n==== N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md ====\n# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,240 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace inspects heat-grid junctions in his ritualistic pre-Piercing routine. He detects an \"off-note\" in the grid hum—pressure deviation signaling potential failure. Bram teases him about working alone and obsessive checking patterns. Ground tremor hits. Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 5: \"Lines That Don't Lie\"**\n",

                "text": "# OMNIMETA SAGA: CONTINUITY LEDGER v1.0\n\n**Date Created:** November 27, 2025  \n**Purpose:** Complete canonical state reference for next conversation  \n**Use:** Read this FIRST before any project work to understand current state\n\n---\n\n## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: WHERE WE ARE\n\n**Project Status:** Phase 1 (Constraint Validation Toolkit) COMPLETE, ready for Phase 2 (N01 Salvage Assessment)\n\n**Turning 1 Status:** N01-N05 exist as \"Generation Zero\" prototypes that predate current constraint systems. Decision made to regenerate from scratch using mature infrastructure.\n\n**Infrastructure Status:** All validation systems production-ready. Story Bible Template complete. Canon frame documents complete. Beat tracking system operational.\n\n**Critical Decision Made This Session:** Full N01-N05 regeneration approved, not retrofitting. Timeline: ~24 weeks for complete Turning 1 reconstruction.\n\n**Next Immediate Action:** Phase 2 - N01 Salvage Assessment (identify which elements to preserve vs regenerate)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: CANONICAL TIME HIERARCHY (CURRENT AUTHORITY)\n\n**CRITICAL: This is the CURRENT CANONICAL hierarchy as of November 27, 2025.**\n\n### Time Units (Smallest to Largest)\n\n**Within-Tide Phase\n",

    "3952685a-b5da-4467-9d17-362661a9d08c": {
      "children": [
        "c957e923-df9e-4c09-be2b-43bc0f7ce681"
      ],
      "id": "3952685a-b5da-4467-9d17-362661a9d08c",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "import pandas as pd, re\ndef get_top_heading(text):\n    for line in text.splitlines():\n        if line.strip().startswith(\"#\"):\n            return line.strip()\n    return None\ndef get_purpose_line(text):\n    for line in text.splitlines()[:60]:\n        if \"PURPOSE\" in line.upper():\n            return line.strip()\n    return None\ndef get_keywords(text):\n    keys=[\"12 chapter\",\"15\",\"validator\",\"beat\",\"interlude\",\"epilogue\",\"gpt\",\"canon\",\"continuity\",\"ledger\",\"template\",\"tracker\",\"grid\",\"mapping\"]\n    found=[k for k in keys if k.lower() in text.lower()]\n    return \", \".join(found[:6])\n\nrows=[]\nfor name in names:\n    text=tar_texts.get(name,\"\")\n    rows.append({\n        \"file\": name.split(\"/\",1)[1],\n        \"bytes\": tar.getmember(name).size,\n        \"top_heading\": get_top_heading(text),\n        \"purpose_line\": get_purpose_line(text),\n        \"keywords\": get_keywords(text)\n    })\ndf=pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(\"file\")\ndf.head(), df.shape\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765726645.451623,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "3952685a-b5da-4467-9d17-362661a9d08c",

      "children": [
        "9cc57c13-162e-4af8-82d5-d7bd0f573c45"
      ],

      "message": {
